inventory.notes: INC-05 RATIFIED (main branch). Laboratory module C7 is complete and shipped with full order lifecycle, no per-analyte result lines (flat columns only: result/report/description/testRange/level/unit). Key design decisions: (1) Separate non-polymorphic LabTest entity (not ClinicalOrder); (2) Settlement flag (settled) is local clinical projection, gated at worklist/open time per CR-INC05-01; cash-PAID settlement seam is deferred but infrastructure in place (ConsultationSettlementListener); (3) Attachment rules (max 5, COLLECTED attach, VERIFIED download, VERIFIED delete-block) enforced at service layer, not DB; (4) Encounter binding is exactly-one (consultation/non-consultation real FK, admission loose uid deferred); (5) Cross-module refs (labTestTypeUid, patientUid, patientBillUid, diagnosis/clinician/insurance uids) are all loose per ADR-0008/ADR-0022; (6) Duplicate guard blocks same labTestTypeUid on same encounter; (7) Rejection with comment, cleared on accept; (8) Hold reverts ACCEPTED→PENDING with audit trail; (9) Verify writes result fields; (10) Result and report are separate fields, both writable post-COLLECTED without status change; (11) Delete PENDING only (credit-note for paid bills deferred). Flyway: V24 (create lab_tests, lab_test_attachments), V33 (patient_id→patient_uid, add settled column). LabTestPort interface is the public intra-module boundary. LabTestService is package-private. All endpoints authenticated-only (no @PreAuthorize per CR-INC05-02 parity). ADR-0022 governs the clinical module. Highest Flyway = V37 (overall), V33 (lab_tests last touch). Ratified decisions in docs/delivery/increments/05-clinical-discovery/11-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md and 17-review-resolutions.md.

inventory.notes: Radiology-specific deltas from LabTest (C7) per CR-INC05-14 + CR-INC05-01: (1) NO collect endpoint or live transition to COLLECTED state — active path is ACCEPTED→VERIFIED DIRECTLY. COLLECTED kept in DB CHECK for legacy data fidelity; collecting via dead endpoint collect_radiology111 (PatientResource.java:4317) is malformed and NOT re-exposed. (2) Result edit gate is ACCEPTED (not COLLECTED) — PatientResource.java:4305-4306 parity; Radiology.saveResult()/RadiologyService.saveResult() and RadiologyService.addAttachment() both gate on ACCEPTED (PatientServiceImpl.java:2931-2933). (3) Attach gate is ACCEPTED (not COLLECTED); max 5 named attachments per order. Download/view gated on VERIFIED (PatientResource.java:6154); delete-attachment blocked when VERIFIED. (4) Inline attachment blob (BYTEA) set at verify time is SEPARATE from named file attachments. (5) Asymmetry: Radiology.accept() does NOT clear rejectComment (verified finding; legacy radiology accept path absent the clear call — exact-process mandate). LabTest.accept() DOES clear it. (6) Settlement flag (local projection): set at order time from ChargeResult (INSURANCE/COVERED/NONE→true, UNPAID→false); cash-PAID→true seam is DEFERRED (same V33 pattern). Worklist uses settled=true filter (CR-INC05-01); eliminates billing bill status read. (7) No admission-scoped endpoints in C8 (admissionUid column exists, loose-ref, check constraint enforced) — deferred to Inpatient/Nursing increment. (8) Delete PENDING: no credit-note raised (deferred seam, same as LabTest). (9) Encounter binding: CHECK num_nonnulls(consultation_id, non_consultation_id, admission_uid)=1 enforced at DB. (10) All endpoints authenticated-only; no additional RBAC gates per CR-INC05-02 parity. Controller owns no @Transactional; service owns transaction boundary per ADR-0014 §5. All cross-module refs are loose uids (no id-FK per ADR-0022 D2 Correction, V34). Highest Flyway = V37. inventory.notes:
PROCEDURE STATE MACHINE: order (PENDING, creates billing charge, sets settled flag from ChargeResult: true for INSURANCE/COVERED, false for CASH) → accept (PENDING|REJECTED → ACCEPTED, NO bill re-check per CR-INC05-01 parity) → add_note (ACCEPTED + settled=true → VERIFIED, DISTINCTIVE mid-transition settlement gate, 422 if settled=false) OR update (ACCEPTED, optional fields, NOT gated on settled) OR delete (PENDING only). NO approve, NO reject, NO hold, NO collect endpoints (planning-doc M14 'approve' is fabricated). REJECTED unreachable at runtime (no reject endpoint), but guard reproduced for completeness. Vestigial held_* columns kept for data fidelity.

SETTLEMENT GATE DISTINCTIVE: add_note is the ONLY transition in the entire order family (LabTest/Radiology/Procedure) that explicitly re-checks the local settled flag BEFORE completion (PatientResource.java:3408-3414 verbatim). LabTest.verify() and Radiology.verify() do NOT re-check settlement; Procedure.addNote() REQUIRES it, throwing 422 'Could not add procedure note. Payment not verified'. This is gated in the domain method Procedure.addNote():512-523.

WORKLIST: settled=true orders in PENDING|ACCEPTED statuses, outpatient+outsider only (no inpatient in legacy). The settled flag (local boolean) replaces reading billing bill status directly (CR-INC05-01, ADR-0022 D4).

BY-PATIENT: CR-INC05-15 DEFERRED — legacy query excludes admission-scoped procedures. Implementation currently returns all procedures per patient since no admission procedures exist in C9. When admission path lands, must filter to consultation+non_consultation only.

THEATREUID HANDLING: Procedure.theatreUid is nullable VARCHAR(26) loose ref (Procedure.java:59-62, no FK, no TheatreLookup validation at order time). Theatre uid is stored as-is without validation; theatre is optional. Theatre masterdata (V6 schema) has no price and no relationships to clinical module.

BILLING SEAM: ConsultationSettlementListener (BEFORE_COMMIT phase, ADR-0022 D5) handles BillSettledEvent and flips procedure.settled=true atomically. Until seam built, CASH-OPD procedure orders are ordered correctly with settled=false, and add_note will be blocked (mirrors LabTest.settled V33 deferral pattern).

SETTLEMENT FLAG LOGIC: isSettledFromCharge(ChargeResult, PaymentMode, inpatient=false) → true if SettlementPolicy.requiresPrepayment() returns false (insurance/covered auto-pass) OR chargeResult.status() != BillStatus.UNPAID (previously paid/verified). For CASH-OPD/CASH-OUTSIDER: requiresPrepayment=true, so settled=true only if bill already PAID.

FLYWAY: V26 (procedures table), V35 (patient_id→patient_uid + settled column). Highest version V37 (closure_patient_uid, post-procedure).

TESTING: ProcedureIT.java (34-56) covers order/consult/non-consult paths, duplicate guard, accept, add_note with settlement gate, add_note with blank note (400), add_note on non-ACCEPTED (422), update, delete, worklist, by-patient, INSURANCE vs CASH, no-approve/reject/hold/collect 404 checks, V35 schema verification, 401 without token.

(Radiology.java:138-378, RadiologyAttachment.java:57-112, V25__clinical_radiologies.sql:121-144, RadiologyController 3 endpoints)

No shared attachment infrastructure or polymorphic base. (V24 and V25 migrations)

Settlement flag - Consultation: settled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE, set at booking per SettlementPolicy (true for INSURANCE/COVERED/follow-up NONE, false for CASH-OPD), flipped to true when cash bill is PAID via ConsultationSettlementListener event seam. Consultation.markSettled() domain method. (Consultation.java:165-181, ConsultationSettlementListener.java:113-117)

Settlement flag - LabTest: settled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE, added V33, set at order time per billing ChargeResult (true for INSURANCE/COVERED/inpatient, false for CASH-OPD), flipped to true when cash bill is PAID via ConsultationSettlementListener event seam. LabTest.markSettled() domain method. Worklist filters settled=true. (LabTest.java:220-241, ConsultationSettlementListener.java:119-123, V33__clinical_lab_tests_patient_uid.sql:63-70)

Settlement flag - Radiology: settled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE, added V34, same pattern as LabTest. Set at order time, flipped to true when cash bill is PAID. Radiology.markSettled() domain method. Worklist filters settled=true. (Radiology.java:239-258, ConsultationSettlementListener.java:125-129, V34__clinical_radiologies_patient_uid.sql:63)

Settlement flag - Procedure: settled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE, added V35, set at order time. DISTINCTIVE gate: add_note transition (ACCEPTED→VERIFIED) re-checks settled==true BEFORE allowing note to be added; throws InvalidPatientOperationException 'Could not add procedure note. Payment not verified' if false. This is the ONLY order transition that re-checks settlement (LabTest/Radiology/Prescription do NOT re-check at verify time). Flipped to true when cash bill is PAID via ConsultationSettlementListener. Procedure.markSettled() domain method. (Procedure.java:260-282, Procedure.addNote():512-523, ConsultationSettlementListener.java:131-135, V35__clinical_procedures_patient_uid.sql)

Settlement flag - Prescription: settled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE, added V36, same pattern as LabTest/Radiology. Set at order time, flipped to true when cash bill is PAID. Prescription.markSettled() domain method. (ConsultationSettlementListener.java:137-141, V36__clinical_prescriptions_patient_uid.sql)

BillSettledEvent: cross-module event record (shared.event.BillSettledEvent) published by billing module when CASH bill transitions to PAID. Carries billUid (ULID) and settledAt (Instant). Published synchronously inside billing transaction, consumed by @TransactionalEventListener(phase=BEFORE_COMMIT) ConsultationSettlementListener within SAME transaction ensuring atomicity. (BillSettledEvent.java:38, ConsultationSettlementListener.java:1-143)

Queries all 5 clinical entity repositories (Consultation, LabTest, Radiology, Procedure, Prescription) by patientBillUid and calls markSettled() on any match found. At most one match per bill (one-to-one cardinality per ADR-0022 D2). If no match found, silently completes (e.g. registration fee bills with no clinical-local projection). inventory.notes:
ATTACHMENTS: No bytea storage — LabTestAttachment and RadiologyAttachment store only filename references (name VARCHAR(200), file_name VARCHAR(400) with global unique). The actual file storage is OUT OF SCOPE per domain comments. Radiology ALSO has an inline attachment BYTEA blob (Radiology.attachment mapped as byte[]) set at verify time, separate from the named attachments table. NO generic Attachment table; entity-specific orphanRemoval=true intra-module child tables. LabTest attachments gate: COLLECTED (attach only, add when status=COLLECTED, delete when NOT VERIFIED, download when VERIFIED). Radiology attachments gate: ACCEPTED (attach when status=ACCEPTED — DISTINCT from LabTest's COLLECTED gate, delete when NOT VERIFIED, download when VERIFIED). PROCEDURE HAS NO ATTACHMENTS.

SETTLEMENT: Every order entity (Consultation, LabTest, Radiology, Procedure, Prescription) carries a local boolean settled flag (NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE). Set at creation time from billing ChargeResult (true for INSURANCE/COVERED/inpatient/NONE, false for CASH-OPD). For CASH, flipped to true when the cash bill transitions to PAID via the ConsultationSettlementListener event seam consuming BillSettledEvent. The event seam is IMPLEMENTED and WIRED (ADR-0022 D5, inc-05 §5) — NOT deferred. Phase=BEFORE_COMMIT ensures atomicity: both the billing PAID transition and the clinical settled flag flip commit or both roll back in the same transaction. Per CR-INC05-01 ruling, settlement is NOT re-checked at order accept/verify time (legacy parity) — EXCEPT Procedure.addNote() which distinctively re-checks settled==true and throws InvalidPatientOperationException if false (the ONLY transition across all 5 entities that re-checks). Worklist queries filter settled=true for LabTest/Radiology. Consultation.open_consultation transition evaluates SettlementPolicy.requireSettled() against the local settled flag (the only settlement gate in the system). The clinical module NEVER calls back into billing for settlement status (ADR-0008 §6).

HIGHEST FLYWAY: V37__clinical_closure_patient_uid.sql (patient_uid loosening on closure tables). Lab/Radiology attachments shipped in V24/V25. Settlement flags shipped in V29 (Consultation), V33 (LabTest), V34 (Radiology), V35 (Procedure), V36 (Prescription). ConsultationSettlementListener component wired in inc-05 application module. ADR-0022 governs cross-module references; all patient/clinic/clinician/bill/medicine/diagnosis-type/admission refs are loose ULID uids (no FK) except the intra-schema Consultation.patient_id/visit_id which remain real FKs (V19 stub already had them; V29 migration converted to uid refs in patient_uid/visit_uid). ApplicationModules.verify() confirms no reverse edge from clinical into billing (only billing→clinical via event seam).

SCOPE: inc-05 shipped on main; clinical module is complete and ratified. Admission-scoped paths (admission_uid columns exist for data fidelity) are deferred to the Inpatient/Nursing increment. NonConsultation walk-in paths are fully implemented. All attachments and settlement mechanisms are live and tested.

**CRITICAL AS-BUILT NOTES FOR AVOIDING RE-BUILD & DUPLICATION:**

1. **Increment 05 (Clinical/OPD) is COMPLETE & SHIPPED on main.** It is NOT a planning doc — it is ratified + merged (PR #11 + QA coverage PR #12). The specification in `05-clinical-discovery/11-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md` is authoritative; the planning doc `05-clinical-opd.md` explicitly states "SUPERSEDED IN PART" and carried "heavy drift" (polymorphic ClinicalOrder, APPROVED procedure state, CONS-/ORD- numbering, ProviderProfile, 177 privilege codes — all inventions).

2. **Critical drift corrections in inc-05 (verify these are NOT re-invented in inc-06+):**
   - Three SEPARATE order tables (lab_tests, radiologies, procedures), NOT polymorphic ClinicalOrder
   - Two SEPARATE diagnosis tables (working_diagnoses, final_diagnoses), NOT one polymorphic entity
   - Consultation states use EXACT legacy spellings: TRANSFERED (single-R), CANCELED (single-L)
   - NO Consultation.COMPLETED state (that's ConsultationTransfer.COMPLETED)
   - Procedure has NO APPROVED state (legacy has none; procedure states PENDING/ACCEPTED/REJECTED/VERIFIED only)
   - Prescription states NOT-GIVEN / GIVEN ONLY (PENDING→...→SOLD lifecycle is Pharmacy inc-08, out of scope)
   - Results stored as COLUMNS (LabTest.result, Radiology.result, Procedure.result), NOT separate OrderResult entity
   - No CONS{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} or ORD{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} numbering (planning-doc invention)

3. **Settlement gate scope (CR-INC05-01 ruling — CRITICAL for inc-06 ordering):**
   - Pay-before-service gate enforced at consultation open_consultation ONLY, NOT at order accept
   - Worklists filter settled=true rows (UI parity)
   - Direct API bypass gap is REPRODUCED (not closed) — legacy gap preserved
   - SettlementDispatcher writes settled=true in SAME tx as PAID (no async, billing→encounter only)
   - Clinical modules read local settled flag ONLY; never call back into billing

4. **ADR-0022 Clinical Ownership Transfer:**
   - Increment 05 builds Consultation as a PENDING-stub in registration context (inc-03)
   - Increment 05 OWNS Consultation permanently (consultation aggregate)
   - All clinical work (notes, exams, orders, diagnoses, transfers, closure) is inc-05
   - Cross-module patient references use ULID uid ONLY (no FK); Flyway V30-V37 backfilled to loose refs
   - Intra-module FK constraints (consultation_id → consultations.id) are RETAINED

5. **Known gaps & deferred items (DO NOT RE-BUILD):**
   - Allergy-check gate at prescribing: DEFERRED as known safety gap (legacy parity; NHIF safety hazard noted)
   - ClinicianPerformance ownership: DEFERRED to inc-10 Reporting / inc-01 IAM (side-effect writes skipped in inc-05)
   - Increment 04 partial-payment (CR-02) + CashierShift OPEN/CLOSED (CR-04) + InsuranceClaim ledger (CR-06): DEFERRED
   - Increment 05 audit DDL (V29 tamper-evident): requirement stated per ADR-0007, DDL deferred to security-architect's PHI classification

6. **Highest Flyway version:** V37 (clinical_closure_patient_uid — final ADR-0022 D2 correction for cross-module ULID refs)

   00 → 01 → 02 → 04 → 03 → 05 → 06 → 08 → 07 → 09 → 10 → 11

Lab "audit" is ONLY the per-action stamp columns on the LabTest row (createdBy/On/At, orderedBy/On/At, acceptedBy/On/At, heldBy/On/At, rejectedBy/On/At, collectedBy/On/At, verifiedBy/On/At — LabTest.java:105-146), each a Long user-id + Long day-id + LocalDateTime, surfaced as concatenated display strings "timestamp | nickname" in LabTestModel (PatientResource.java:3850-3880).
2. DEVICE-FINGERPRINT / DEVICE-BINDING: No device-fingerprint or device-binding feature exists in the legacy lab code or security filters — grep for fingerprint/deviceId/device_id/deviceBinding/userAgent across com.orbix.api returned ZERO files. Agents must not treat this as a feature to preserve.

STATE MACHINE (LabTest.status is a free-text String column, LabTest.java:55; NO enum). Six distinct status values exist. Initial status PENDING is set in the service, NOT the controller (PatientServiceImpl.java:819). Valid transitions and their guards are itemized in capabilities. HOLD is NOT a terminal status — holding an ACCEPTED test sets status back to "PENDING" while stamping heldBy/heldOn/heldAt (PatientResource.java:4017-4021). There is NO "COMPLETED" status and NO "CANCELLED" status on a LabTest in legacy. There is NO PENDING->COMPLETED path because COMPLETED does not exist; the legacy terminal verified state is "VERIFIED".

CONFIDENCE HIGH: every claim cited to exact file:line; controller, service, entity, DTO and planning doc all read in full for the lab paths.

capabilities.name: Lab test ordering (create) — guards & dual-table billing creation
capabilities.description: Endpoint POST /patients/save_lab_test (PatientResource.java:1931-1972). Controller-level guards: looks up consultation_id/non_consultation_id/admission_id. If consultation present and patient.type != 'OUTPATIENT' -> InvalidOperationException 'Patient is not an outpatient. Please reload patient to continue with this operation.' (PatientResource.java:1945-1946). If consultation present and labTestRepository.existsByConsultationAndLabTestType -> 'Duplicate Lab Test Types is not allowed' (PatientResource.java:1948-1949). For nonConsultation: patient.type must == 'OUTSIDER' else 'Patient is not an outsider. Please reload patient to continue with this operation.' (PatientResource.java:1952-1953); duplicate guard same message (PatientResource.java:1955-1956). On new (id==null): controller stamps createdBy/On/At and orderedBy/On/At from current user + dayService (PatientResource.java:1960-1968). Service saveLabTest (PatientServiceImpl.java:747): exactly-one-of {consultation,nonConsultation,admission} rule enforced by 5 explicit branches — more than one -> 'Could not save, labtest should not have more than two properties' (PatientServiceImpl.java:768,771,774,777); none -> 'Could not save, labtest must have one property' (PatientServiceImpl.java:780). NonConsultation PENDING is auto-promoted to 'IN-PROCESS' on first order (PatientServiceImpl.java:790-792); if already signed off -> 'Could not be done. Patient already signed off' (PatientServiceImpl.java:796). Admission PENDING -> 'Could not be done. Admission not verified' (PatientServiceImpl.java:805); non IN-PROCESS -> 'Could not be done. Patient already signed off/ or discharged' (PatientServiceImpl.java:809). Status set to 'PENDING' (PatientServiceImpl.java:819). NOTE the service RETURNS null after labTestRepository.save(test) (PatientServiceImpl.java:986-987) — the controller wraps null in ResponseEntity.created.

capabilities.name: Order-time billing math & insurance/COVERED logic (PatientBill + PatientInvoice + PatientInvoiceDetail)
capabilities.description: On order a PatientBill is created (PatientServiceImpl.java:821-835): amount = labTestType.getPrice() (cash price, a double — LabTestType.java:56), paid=0, balance=price, qty=1, billItem='Lab Test', description='Lab Test: '+labTestType.getName(), status='UNPAID', patient set, saved. THEN: if patient.getPaymentType()=='INSURANCE' OR admission present (PatientServiceImpl.java:837): looks up LabTestTypeInsurancePlan by (labTestType, patient.insurancePlan, covered=true) (PatientServiceImpl.java:839). If a covered plan price exists: bill.amount = plan price, paid = plan price, balance = 0, status='COVERED', paymentType='INSURANCE', membershipNo = patient.membershipNo, insurancePlan set (PatientServiceImpl.java:841-849); a PENDING PatientInvoice for (patient, insurancePlan) is reused or created (No initially set to String.valueOf(Math.random()) then overwritten with the invoice id.toString() — PatientServiceImpl.java:857,878,926,947), and a PatientInvoiceDetail is added (amount=bill.amount, description='Lab Test: '+name, qty=1) (PatientServiceImpl.java:883-894 / 899-910). ELSE if admission present but NO covered plan: bill kept at cash price, paid=0, balance=price, status='VERIFIED' (PatientServiceImpl.java:912-918), attached to a PENDING PatientInvoice with insurancePlan=null and a PatientInvoiceDetail (PatientServiceImpl.java:920-980). Pure cash outpatient with paymentType != INSURANCE leaves bill status='UNPAID'. Price is locked at order time; no recompute at later transitions.

capabilities.name: Accept transition (PENDING/REJECTED -> ACCEPTED)
capabilities.description: POST /patients/accept_lab_test (PatientResource.java:3888-3912). Body is LLabTest (id only used). Guard: status must be PENDING or REJECTED else InvalidOperationException 'Could not accept, only PENDING or REJECTED tests can be accepted' (PatientResource.java:3896-3897). Sets acceptedBy/On/At; CLEARS rejectedBy/On/At and sets rejectComment to empty string '' (PatientResource.java:3904-3907); status='ACCEPTED'. Returns boolean true. NOTE: accept does NOT check the PatientBill payment status — payment gating for lab is enforced only at the worklist queue level and at report/result entry, not at accept.

capabilities.name: Reject transition (PENDING/ACCEPTED -> REJECTED) + reject reason
capabilities.description: POST /patients/reject_lab_test (PatientResource.java:3914-3937). Guard: status must be PENDING or ACCEPTED else 'Could not accept, only PENDING or ACCEPTED tests can be rejected' (PatientResource.java:3922-3923) — note the message text begins 'Could not accept' (copy-paste artifact) though the action is reject. Sets rejectedBy/On/At; CLEARS acceptedBy/On/At; status='REJECTED'. Separate endpoint POST /patients/lab_tests/save_reason_for_rejection (PatientResource.java:2034-2048) sets rejectComment but ONLY when current status=='REJECTED' else 'Could not save. Only allowed for rejected tests' (PatientResource.java:2046).

capabilities.name: Collect transition (ACCEPTED -> COLLECTED)
capabilities.description: POST /patients/collect_lab_test (PatientResource.java:3939-3958). Guard: status must be exactly ACCEPTED else 'Could not accept, only ACCEPTED tests can be collected' (PatientResource.java:3947-3948) — again message text reads 'Could not accept'. Sets collectedBy/On/At; status='COLLECTED'. COLLECTED is the only state in which attachments may be uploaded and in which results may be saved/updated.

capabilities.name: Save/update results (COLLECTED only, no verify)
capabilities.description: POST /patients/save_lab_test_results (PatientResource.java:3985-4003). Guard: status must be COLLECTED else 'Could not update, only COLLECTED tests can be saved/updated' (PatientResource.java:3993-3994). Copies result, level, range, unit verbatim from client LLabTest DTO (PatientResource.java:3996-3999). Does NOT change status (stays COLLECTED). NO server-side reference-range evaluation, NO flag/NORMAL/HIGH/LOW computation anywhere — range and level are free-text strings supplied by the client (LLabTest fields, PatientResource.java:6427-6433; LabTest.range/level are plain String, LabTest.java:51-53).

capabilities.name: Verify transition (COLLECTED -> VERIFIED) — terminal
capabilities.description: POST /patients/verify_lab_test (PatientResource.java:3960-3983). Guard: status must be COLLECTED else 'Could not verify, only COLLECTED tests can be verified' (PatientResource.java:3968-3969). Copies result, level, range, unit from client (PatientResource.java:3971-3974), stamps verifiedBy/On/At, sets status='VERIFIED'. VERIFIED is the terminal lab state in legacy (there is NO 'COMPLETED'). Once VERIFIED, attachments can no longer be deleted (see attachment guard).

capabilities.name: Hold action (ACCEPTED -> PENDING, with held stamp)
capabilities.description: POST /patients/hold_lab_test (PatientResource.java:4005-4024). Guard: status must be exactly ACCEPTED else 'Could not hold, only ACCEPTED tests can be held' (PatientResource.java:4013-4014). Stamps heldBy/On/At then sets status back to 'PENDING' (PatientResource.java:4017-4021). HOLD is NOT a status value — it is an ACCEPTED->PENDING reversal that records who/when it was held. The held stamp survives and is displayed (LabTestModel.held, PatientResource.java:3871-3874).

capabilities.name: Report entry (free text, payment-gated)
capabilities.description: POST /patients/lab_tests/add_report (PatientResource.java:3381-3395). Sets LabTest.report (a length-10000 String column, LabTest.java:48-49) only if the PatientBill.status is PAID or COVERED or VERIFIED else 'Could not add report. Payment not verified' (PatientResource.java:3389,3393). This is the only place report text is written. This guard reads the BILL status (not the lab test status), so a report can be added at any lab status as long as the bill is paid/covered/verified.

capabilities.name: Worklist queues (3 separate endpoints by patient class) — payment gate is in the query, not the transition
capabilities.description: Three GET worklist endpoints return distinct Patient lists (de-duplicated via HashSet): get_lab_outpatient_list (PatientResource.java:3668-3683) — consultations with status 'IN-PROCESS', includes patient if type=='OUTPATIENT' AND bill.status in {PAID, COVERED}. get_lab_inpatient_list (PatientResource.java:3685-3700) — admissions 'IN-PROCESS', type=='INPATIENT' AND bill.status in {PAID, COVERED, VERIFIED} (note inpatient additionally allows VERIFIED). get_lab_outsider_list (PatientResource.java:3702-3717) — nonConsultations 'IN-PROCESS', type=='OUTSIDER' AND bill.status in {PAID, COVERED}. The payment gate is implemented ONLY as this worklist inclusion filter — there is no settled-flag precondition inside accept()/complete(). Per-patient lab tests for the worklist drill-down: get_lab_tests_by_patient_id (PatientResource.java:3804-3886) returns only tests whose bill.status in {PAID, COVERED, VERIFIED} (PatientResource.java:3820).

capabilities.name: Attachments (per-lab-test, dedicated table, max 5, COLLECTED-only, app-level filename)
capabilities.description: Upload POST /patients/upload_lab_test_attachment (PatientResource.java:5941-5956 -> PatientServiceImpl.java:2823). Guards: max 5 attachments per lab test else 'Can not add more than 5 attachments' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2828-2829); lab test status must be COLLECTED else 'Can only attach for collected tests' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2832-2833); file size max 10485760L bytes (10 MB) else 'File exceeds maximum file size allowed' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2836-2839). Stored on local filesystem at CompanyProfile.publicPath (PatientServiceImpl.java:2846-2882); requires non-null/non-empty publicPath else 'Driver not found. Contact (System) Administrator' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2855-2860). Filename generated app-side (NOT a sequence): 'LT' + labTest.id + patient.no + Math.random() + LocalDateTime.now(), stripped of / . : - then '.'+extension (PatientServiceImpl.java:2873-2877); stored unique in lab_test_attachments.fileName (LabTestAttachment.java:43-45). createdBy is set to labTest.getCollectedBy() (PatientServiceImpl.java:2902). Download GET /patients/download_lab_test_attachment streams bytes inline by file_name (PatientResource.java:5960-6007). Delete POST /patients/delete_lab_test_attachment (PatientResource.java:6010-6025): blocked if owning lab test status=='VERIFIED' -> 'Could not delete. Lab Test already verified' (PatientResource.java:6021-6022). Entity LabTestAttachment is a DEDICATED table 'lab_test_attachments' with ManyToOne to LabTest (LabTestAttachment.java:34-50); LabTest.labTestAttachments is OneToMany orphanRemoval=true EAGER (LabTest.java:149-153).

capabilities.name: Delete lab test (PENDING-only) with credit-note / invoice reversal
capabilities.description: POST /patients/delete_lab_test (PatientResource.java:2912-2965). Guard: status must be PENDING else 'Could not delete, only a PENDING lab test can be deleted' (PatientResource.java:2917-2918). Reversal logic: if a PatientPaymentDetail for the bill exists with status 'RECEIVED', a PatientCreditNote is created (amount=bill.amount, reference='Canceled lab test', status='PENDING', No from patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo()) (PatientResource.java:2924-2939). Any PatientInvoiceDetail for the bill is deleted; if its parent PatientInvoice then has zero details the invoice is deleted (PatientResource.java:2940-2954 — note the detail-count loop uses 'j = j++' which is a no-op bug, so j stays 0 and the invoice is always deleted when a detail existed). Any PatientPaymentDetail is deleted (PatientResource.java:2955-2959), then the LabTest and PatientBill are deleted (PatientResource.java:2960-2961).

capabilities.name: Cascade cleanup of lab tests on consultation/non-consultation cancel & sign-off
capabilities.description: On patient/consultation cancel paths, PENDING lab tests with UNPAID (or null-status) bills are hard-deleted along with their bills (PatientResource.java:434-451). On consultation sign-off, lab-test bills that are UNPAID are flipped to 'CANCELED' (PatientResource.java:701-711). On referral-plan / sign-off save, an UNPAID lab-test bill blocks the operation -> 'Could not save. Patient have uncleared lab test bill(s)' (PatientResource.java:5465-5472 and 5634-5641).

capabilities.name: Lab test status display strings & returned model
capabilities.description: LabTestModel (models/LabTestModel.java:38-67) exposes result/report/description/range/level/unit/status plus 8 audit display strings (created/ordered/accepted/held/rejected/rejectComment/collected/verified) built as '<timestamp> | <user nickname>' (rejected additionally appends ' | <rejectComment>') (PatientResource.java:3850-3880). NOTE: LabTest entity has paymentType and membershipNo columns (LabTest.java:57-58) but LabTestModel does NOT map them — they are not surfaced to the worklist UI. loadLabTests (PatientResource.java:2254-2305) and getAllLabTestsByPatient (PatientResource.java:4804-4885) provide alternative read views.

absentInLegacy: NO 'COMPLETED' status for lab tests. Planning doc (06-...md:38,77,96) claims Lab lifecycle 'PENDING -> ACCEPTED -> COMPLETED'. Legacy terminal status is 'VERIFIED' (PatientResource.java:3980), reached from 'COLLECTED', and there is an intermediate 'COLLECTED' state the doc omits. Real legacy machine: PENDING -> ACCEPTED -> COLLECTED -> VERIFIED, with REJECTED branch and HOLD reversal. Doc collapses COLLECTED+VERIFIED into a single 'COMPLETED' that does not exist.

NO 'CANCELLED' status on a LabTest. Planning doc (06-...md:38) lists '(/ CANCELLED)'. A LabTest has no CANCELLED status; it is hard-deleted (PatientResource.java:2960) and only its PatientBill can become 'CANCELED' (PatientResource.java:706). There is no LabTest.status=='CANCELLED' code path anywhere.

NO COLLECTED state in the planning doc, and NO HOLD action. Legacy has both: 'COLLECTED' (PatientResource.java:3955) and the hold endpoint that reverts ACCEPTED->PENDING with held stamp (PatientResource.java:4005-4024). The doc's 3-state model loses specimen-collection and hold semantics entirely.

NO server-side reference-range flag computation. Planning doc item 6 (06-...md:63) claims LabResultService.upsertLines computes NORMAL/LOW/HIGH/CRITICAL_LOW/CRITICAL_HIGH server-side from MdReferenceRange by (analyteUid,sex,age). Legacy stores 'range' and 'level' as free-text Strings taken verbatim from the client LLabTest DTO (PatientResource.java:3971-3974, 3996-3999; LabTest.java:51-53). There is NO per-analyte LabResultLine entity and NO flag enum in legacy — a lab test holds a single result/range/level/unit on the LabTest row itself.

NO LabResultLine / per-analyte result rows. Planning doc (06-...md:14) introduces LabResultLine linked to MdLabTestAnalyte. Legacy has one flat result per LabTest (LabTest.result, LabTest.java:47) — there is no analyte breakdown entity. (LabTestTypeRange exists on LabTestType as reference master data only, LabTestType.java:60-64, and is never used to evaluate results.)

NO LabBatch / batch grouping / bulk accept-complete. Planning doc (06-...md:15,26,27,65,94) introduces LabBatch grouping orders by (labTestTypeUid, orderDate) with atomic bulk accept/complete. ABSENT in legacy: no batch entity, no batch endpoints, no bulk transition — every lab test is accepted/collected/verified individually (PatientResource.java:3888-4024). Confirmed no batching anywhere in LabTestRepository.java.

NO lab document/batch numbering scheme. Planning doc items 10 & DoD (06-...md:71,94) require a 'seq_lab_batch_no' sequence producing 'LABB{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}'. ABSENT in legacy: a LabTest has only an auto-increment DB id (LabTest.java:44-46); there is no human-facing lab order number, no prefix, no zero-padding, no fiscal reset. The only generated string is the attachment FILENAME (PatientServiceImpl.java:2873-2877) which is random+timestamp, not a sequence.

NO 'settled' flag / SettlementDispatcher / urn:hmis:error:ORDER_NOT_SETTLED gate on accept/complete. Planning doc items 1 & DoD (06-...md:53,95) require accept()/complete() to assert clinicalOrder.settled==true for CASH. Legacy enforces payment ONLY as a worklist inclusion filter (bill.status in PAID/COVERED[/VERIFIED], PatientResource.java:3676,3693,3710) and at report entry (PatientResource.java:3389); the accept/collect/verify endpoints perform NO bill/payment check at all (PatientResource.java:3896,3947,3968).

NO unified ServicePrice matrix / PriceLookup.resolve with cash fallback. Planning doc item 8 (06-...md:42,67) describes a unified ServicePrice(planUid,kind,serviceUid). Legacy pricing is two separate sources: LabTestType.price (cash double, LabTestType.java:56) and LabTestTypeInsurancePlan.price keyed by (labTestType, insurancePlan, covered=true) (LabTestTypeInsurancePlan.java; lookup at PatientServiceImpl.java:839). There is no generic kind-discriminated price table.

NO generic ADR-0015 'attachment' table with (ownerKind=CLINICAL_ORDER, ownerUid). Planning doc (06-...md:18,81) forbids a lab_test_attachment table and mandates a generic one. The legacy system DOES use a dedicated 'lab_test_attachments' table (LabTestAttachment.java:34) — i.e., legacy is exactly the narrow per-type scope the doc says to avoid. There is no generic attachment aggregate, no ownerKind/ownerUid, no StoragePort/MinIO/S3, no ClamAV scan, no pre-signed redirect; legacy stores files on local disk at CompanyProfile.publicPath (PatientServiceImpl.java:2863) and streams inline (PatientResource.java:5993-5995).

NO ClinicalOrder polymorphic aggregate with kind discriminator. Planning doc (06-...md:13) unifies lab/radiology/procedure into one ClinicalOrder. Legacy keeps three fully separate entities/tables (LabTest, Radiology, Procedure) each with its own status string and its own endpoints in PatientResource.java.

NO @PreAuthorize / RBAC on any lab endpoint. Planning doc item & DoD (06-...md:46,99) require @PreAuthorize('hasAuthority('LAB_TEST-...')') on every endpoint. In legacy every lab endpoint's @PreAuthorize is COMMENTED OUT (e.g. PatientResource.java:1932, 5942, 5961, 6011) — there is no method-level privilege gating on lab order/result/attachment operations at all.

NO audit events (LabOrderAccepted/LabOrderCompleted etc.) and NO Envers. Planning doc DoD (06-...md:100) requires Spring Modulith @ApplicationModuleListener audit events. ABSENT in legacy: no domain events, no Envers @Audited (zero matches in com.orbix.api). Lab history is only the stamp columns on the LabTest row.

NO theatre/operative-record/surgeon-approval for procedures-as-related-to-lab (out of lab scope but relevant to the same increment): per MEMORY and TheatreResource, Theatre is master-data only; OperativeRecord/approve()/schedule() (06-...md:17,30-33,57-59) are ABSENT in legacy. (Lab tests themselves have no approval step — only PENDING/ACCEPTED, never an APPROVED status.)

- PENDING -> REJECTED  via /patients/reject_radiology (guard: status in {PENDING, ACCEPTED}) [PatientResource.java:4226-4248]
- ACCEPTED -> REJECTED via same reject endpoint [PatientResource.java:4234-4246]
- ACCEPTED -> PENDING  via /patients/hold_radiology (NOTE: "hold" sets status back to PENDING, NOT a distinct HELD state; guard: status==ACCEPTED) [PatientResource.java:4251-4269]
- ACCEPTED -> VERIFIED via /patients/verify_radiology (guard: status==ACCEPTED) [PatientResource.java:4272-4294]
- ACCEPTED -> COLLECTED via /patients/collect_radiology111 (guard: status==ACCEPTED) — DEAD/orphan endpoint, see capability note [PatientResource.java:4317-4335]
accept() clears reject fields (rejectedby/On/At=null, rejectComment="") [4216-4219]; reject() clears accept fields [4242-4244]; accept() clears nothing about held; hold() stamps heldby/On/At but reverts status to PENDING [4263-4267]. There is NO "report"/"COMPLETED"/"APPROVED"/"SCHEDULED"/"HELD" status value anywhere; the only persisted radiology statuses are PENDING, ACCEPTED, REJECTED, VERIFIED, COLLECTED.

PHI/PII: only field names and types referenced; no patient data reproduced.

Early-discovery findings (must be carried in every audit/auth spec): (1) No Hibernate Envers audit trail is effectively active in the legacy system — zero @Audited annotations across com.orbix.api (grep count 0). Downstream agents must not assume an Envers audit baseline exists. capabilities.name: Order creation (save_radiology) — duplicate guard, patient-type guard, ownership exclusivity
capabilities.description: Endpoint POST /patients/save_radiology. Resolves consultation/nonConsultation/admission by id. Guards (VERBATIM messages): if consultation present and patient.type != 'OUTPATIENT' -> 'Patient is not an outpatient. Please reload patient to continue with this operation.'; duplicate same RadiologyType on same consultation -> 'Duplicate Radiology Types is not allowed'; for nonConsultation patient.type must == 'OUTSIDER' else 'Patient is not an outsider. Please reload patient to continue with this operation.', duplicate -> 'Duplicate Radiology Types is not allowed'. On new (id==null) the controller stamps createdBy/On/At AND orderedby/On/At = current user/day/timestamp. Service-side exclusivity: exactly ONE of consultation/nonConsultation/admission must be present; any combo of two-or-three -> 'Could not save, radiology should not have more than two properties' (note the message wording is misleading; it fires for 2 or 3); none present -> 'Could not save, radiology should have one property'. NonConsultation PENDING->IN-PROCESS side effect on order; if NC status not PENDING/IN-PROCESS -> 'Could not be done. Patient already signed off'. Admission must be IN-PROCESS; PENDING admission -> 'Could not be done. Admission not verified'; other -> 'Could not be done. Patient already signed off'. Sets radio.status='PENDING'. No @PreAuthorize is active (commented out at line 1975).

capabilities.name: Billing math at order-raise (PatientBill / PatientInvoice / PatientInvoiceDetail creation)
capabilities.description: In PatientServiceImpl.saveRadiology a PatientBill is created with amount=RadiologyType.price (double), paid=0, balance=price, qty=1, billItem='Radiology', description='Radiology: '+RadiologyType.name, status='UNPAID'. Then branch on patient.getPaymentType()=='INSURANCE' OR admission present (a.isPresent()). INSURANCE branch: lookup RadiologyTypeInsurancePlan by (radiologyType, patient.insurancePlan, covered=true); if found -> bill.amount=plan.price, paid=plan.price, balance=0, status='COVERED', paymentType='INSURANCE', membershipNo=patient.membershipNo, insurancePlan=plan.insurancePlan; then attaches to existing PENDING PatientInvoice for the patient or creates a new one (PatientInvoice.no first set to String.valueOf(Math.random()) then overwritten with the generated id.toString()); a PatientInvoiceDetail(amount=bill.amount, description='Radiology: '+name, qty=1) is saved. ADMISSION branch (a.isPresent() && no covered plan): bill.amount=price, paid=0, balance=price, status='VERIFIED'; invoice lookup uses findByPatientAndInsurancePlanAndStatus(patient, null, 'PENDING'); creates/updates a no-plan PatientInvoice + PatientInvoiceDetail identically. CASH path (paymentType != INSURANCE and no admission) leaves bill at UNPAID with no invoice. price is a Java double (RadiologyType.java:50; RadiologyTypeInsurancePlan.java:42) — pre-approved migration to BigDecimal.
capabilities.citation: PatientServiceImpl.java:1063-1225 (bill 1063-1075; insurance-covered 1077-1151; admission-uninsured 1152-1221; cash leaves UNPAID); RadiologyType.java:50; RadiologyTypeInsurancePlan.java:36,42-44

capabilities.name: Accept order (accept_radiology)
capabilities.description: POST /patients/accept_radiology, body LRadiology (id). NotFound -> 'Radiology not found'. Guard: status must be PENDING or REJECTED else InvalidOperationException 'Could not accept, only PENDING or REJECTED radiologies can be accepted'. Stamps acceptedby/On/At; nulls rejectedby/On/At and sets rejectComment=''; sets status='ACCEPTED'. Returns boolean true. No @PreAuthorize active.

capabilities.name: Reject order (reject_radiology)
capabilities.description: POST /patients/reject_radiology. NotFound -> 'Radiology not found'. Guard: status must be PENDING or ACCEPTED else 'Could not accept, only PENDING or ACCEPTED radiologies can be rejected' (NOTE the verb 'accept' in this reject message is a legacy copy-paste). Stamps rejectedby/On/At; nulls acceptedby/On/At; sets status='REJECTED'. Reject comment is NOT set here — it is set via a separate endpoint /patients/radiologies/save_reason_for_rejection which only works when status=='REJECTED' (else 'Could not save. Only allowed for rejected tests'; NotFound -> 'Radiology not found').

capabilities.name: Hold order (hold_radiology) — reverts to PENDING, not a HELD state
capabilities.description: POST /patients/hold_radiology. NotFound -> 'Radiology not found'. Guard: status must == ACCEPTED else 'Could not hold, only ACCEPTED radiologies can be held'. Stamps heldby/On/At but sets status back to 'PENDING' (there is NO 'HELD' status literal). Entity has held_by/on/at columns (Radiology.java:127-131) populated, but the workflow status returns to PENDING.

capabilities.name: Verify result (verify_radiology) — terminal documented result state
capabilities.description: POST /patients/verify_radiology, body LRadiology. NotFound -> 'Radiology not found'. Guard: status must == ACCEPTED else 'Could not verify, only ACCEPTED radiologies can be verified'. Sets result=radio.result, attachment=radio.attachment (diagnosisType/description setters are COMMENTED OUT at 4284-4285), stamps verifiedby/On/At, sets status='VERIFIED'. This is the de-facto terminal completed state for radiology.

capabilities.name: Save result without verifying (save_radiology_results)
capabilities.description: POST /patients/save_radiology_results. NotFound -> 'Radiology not found'. Guard: status must == ACCEPTED else 'Could not update, only ACCEPTED radiologies can be updated'. Sets result and attachment only (diagnosisType/description commented out 4309-4310); does NOT change status (stays ACCEPTED). Allows incremental result entry before final verify.

capabilities.name: DEAD endpoint collect_radiology111
capabilities.description: POST /patients/collect_radiology111 maps Radiology.id. NotFound -> 'Radiology not found'. Guard: status must == ACCEPTED else 'Could not accept, only ACCEPTED radiologies can be collected' (again 'accept' verb is legacy copy-paste). Stamps collectedby/On/At, sets status='COLLECTED'. The '111' URL suffix strongly indicates a disabled/orphaned route; no other lifecycle path produces or consumes COLLECTED, and load/list models never branch on COLLECTED. Treat as ABSENT functionally (dead code) — collected_by/on/at columns exist (Radiology.java:133-137) but are never read elsewhere.

capabilities.name: Add radiologist report (radiologies/add_report) — gated on BILL status, not order status
capabilities.description: POST /patients/radiologies/add_report, body Radiology(id, report). NotFound -> 'Radiology not found'. Guard is on the PatientBill status, not the Radiology status: allowed only when patientBill.status in {PAID, COVERED, VERIFIED}; sets Radiology.report (column length 10000, Radiology.java:47-48). Otherwise InvalidOperationException 'Could not add report. Payment not verified'. This is the legacy free-text report mechanism (single String field on Radiology, NOT a separate RadiologyReport entity).

capabilities.name: Delete order (delete_radiology) — PENDING-only, credit-note + invoice cleanup
capabilities.description: POST /patients/delete_radiology?id=. Guard: status must == PENDING else 'Could not delete, only a PENDING radiology can be deleted' (NOTE: no isPresent check before r.get() at 3423 — would NPE on bad id). If a PatientPaymentDetail exists with status=='RECEIVED', creates a PatientCreditNote(amount=bill.amount, patient, reference='Canceled radiology', status='PENDING', no from patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo()). Deletes any PatientInvoiceDetail for the bill; if its parent PatientInvoice then has zero details it deletes the PatientInvoice (NOTE: the detail-count loop uses 'j = j++' at line 3455 which is a no-op bug — j always stays 0, so the empty-invoice delete branch effectively always runs). Deletes the PatientPaymentDetail if present (the 'cannot delete paid radiology' guard is COMMENTED OUT at 3463). Finally deletes Radiology then PatientBill.
capabilities.citation: PatientResource.java:3418-3471 (guard 3423-3424; credit note 3431-3445; invoice cleanup 3446-3460 incl. j=j++ bug at 3455; commented guard 3463)

capabilities.name: Worklist / patient lists (get_radiologies_by_patient_id) — payment gate is a LIST FILTER
capabilities.description: GET /patients/get_radiologies_by_patient_id?patient_no= collates radiologies across all consultations+nonConsultations+admissions of the patient and INCLUDES a radiology in the returned list only if its PatientBill.status in {PAID, COVERED, VERIFIED} (line 4134). This payment gate is implemented purely as a list/visibility filter, NOT as a precondition on accept/verify. Per-row it composes display strings 'created/ordered/rejected/accepted/held/verified' as '<timestamp> | <nickname>[ | <rejectComment> for rejected]'. Separate raw lists exist: get_radiology_outsider_list (4119-...) / outpatient / inpatient by nonConsultation/consultation/admission. load_radiologies (2348-2427) returns all radiologies for one encounter with NO payment filter, mapping status verbatim.

capabilities.name: Attachments (radiology_attachments table) — upload/download/delete with guards
capabilities.description: Upload POST /patients/upload_radiology_attachment (multipart file + radiology_id + name): NotFound -> 'Radiology not found'. Service guards: max 5 attachments -> 'Can not add more than 5 attachments'; radiology.status must == ACCEPTED else 'Can only attach for accepted tests'; file size > 10485760 (10 MiB) -> 'File exceeds maximum file size allowed'; CompanyProfile/publicPath checks -> 'Company Profile not found' / 'Driver not found. Contact Administrator' / 'Driver not found. Contact System Administrator'. Stored filename = ('RAD'+id+patient.no+Math.random()+LocalDateTime.now()) stripped of / . : - plus original extension; persisted to a dedicated RadiologyAttachment row (table 'radiology_attachments', file stored on local CompanyProfile.publicPath disk). Download GET /patients/download_radiology_attachment?file_name= streams from disk inline. Delete POST /patients/delete_radiology_attachment?attachment_id=: NotFound -> 'Attachment not found'; guard radiology.status=='VERIFIED' -> 'Could not delete. Radiology already verified' (gate is on VERIFIED, not COMPLETED). No @PreAuthorize active on any of these.
capabilities.citation: PatientResource.java:6074-6089 (upload ctrl), 6093-6140 (download), 6143-6157 (delete guard at 6154-6156); PatientServiceImpl.java:2922-2996 (guards 2927-2939, filename 2972-2976); RadiologyAttachment.java:27 (table name)

capabilities.name: Encounter-level uncleared-bill blocker referencing radiology
capabilities.description: When signing off / saving certain encounter operations, the code iterates radiologies of the consultation and if any radiology.patientBill.status=='UNPAID' throws InvalidOperationException 'Could not save. Patient have uncleared radiology test bill(s)'. Also during certain cleanup paths (e.g. nonConsultation re-open at 453-468 and consultation handling at 715-720) PENDING radiologies with UNPAID bills are deleted along with their PatientBill.

capabilities.name: Numbering scheme — radiology has NO document number; only credit-note number is generated
capabilities.description: Radiology orders carry NO human-facing document/order number — identity is the DB IDENTITY primary key only (Radiology.java:43-45). PatientInvoice.no is set to id.toString() (after a transient Math.random() placeholder) at PatientServiceImpl.java:1118-1119 and 1187-1188. The only generated number in the radiology flow is the PatientCreditNote.no via patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo() on cancellation (PatientResource.java:3438). There is NO 'LABB{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}' or any radiology batch/sequence in legacy.

absentInLegacy: Three sibling modules / a polymorphic ClinicalOrder aggregate with a kind discriminator (LAB_TEST/RADIOLOGY/PROCEDURE): ABSENT. Legacy radiology is a standalone Radiology entity (domain/Radiology.java) with no discriminator and no shared ClinicalOrder; it is wired entirely inside PatientResource.java.

A 'COMPLETED' status for radiology: ABSENT. The doc's lifecycle 'PENDING -> ACCEPTED -> COMPLETED (/ CANCELLED)' does not match legacy. Legacy result-completion state is literally 'VERIFIED' (PatientResource.java:4292); there is no COMPLETED and no CANCELLED status string — cancellation is a hard delete (delete_radiology), and a separate dead 'COLLECTED' state exists.

A radiology 'complete()' endpoint with an ACCEPTED->COMPLETED guard: ABSENT. Legacy has verify_radiology (ACCEPTED->VERIFIED) and save_radiology_results (stays ACCEPTED); no /radiology/orders/uid/{uid}/complete equivalent.

CASH payment gate enforced as a hard precondition on accept()/complete() returning HTTP 409 urn:hmis:error:ORDER_NOT_SETTLED: ABSENT for radiology. accept_radiology/verify_radiology/save_radiology_results impose NO bill-status check whatsoever (PatientResource.java:4208,4234,4280,4305 guard only on order status). The only payment gating is (a) add_report requiring bill in {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED} (3191), (b) get_radiologies_by_patient_id as a LIST filter (4134), and (c) encounter sign-off blocking on UNPAID radiology bills (5477,5646). There is no 'settled' boolean and no SettlementDispatcher.

A separate RadiologyReport entity 'attached after images are uploaded': ABSENT. The legacy report is a single String field Radiology.report (length 10000), set via add_report and gated only on PatientBill status (PatientResource.java:3191-3193; Radiology.java:47-48).
An OperativeRecord and surgeon APPROVED state: N/A to radiology (those are procedure concepts) — but note the doc bundles them in the same increment; for radiology there is no approve step at all.

Theatre scheduling fields/endpoints on radiology orders (theatreUid/scheduledAt/schedule endpoint): ABSENT for radiology. schedule applies only to procedures in the doc; legacy radiology has no scheduling at all.

Generic ADR-0015 'attachment' table keyed (ownerKind=CLINICAL_ORDER, ownerUid) with MinIO/S3 + ClamAV + pre-signed redirects: ABSENT. Legacy uses a dedicated 'radiology_attachments' table (RadiologyAttachment.java:27), local-disk storage at CompanyProfile.publicPath, plain inline streaming, max 5 attachments, 10 MiB cap, and an ACCEPTED-status upload guard — none of which the doc captures. The doc's instruction 'do not create a radiology_attachment table' is a deliberate modernization, not legacy behaviour.

Attachment delete guard citation 'PatientResource.java lines 6021-6023 confirmed' is MISCITED: 6021-6023 is the LAB TEST attachment delete guard ('Could not delete. Lab Test already verified'). The RADIOLOGY attachment delete guard is at PatientResource.java:6154-6156 ('Could not delete. Radiology already verified'). Both gate on status=='VERIFIED', not the doc's 'COMPLETED or CANCELLED'.

seq_lab_batch_no / LABB{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} document numbers and any LabBatch-style batch grouping for radiology: ABSENT. Radiology orders have no document number at all and no batch concept; identity is the DB IDENTITY id only.

Pricing locked via a unified ServicePrice(planUid,kind,serviceUid,currency) matrix with PriceLookup.resolve(): ABSENT as such. Legacy resolves price from RadiologyType.price (cash, a double) or RadiologyTypeInsurancePlan.price when (radiologyType, plan, covered=true) matches (PatientServiceImpl.java:1079-1082); price IS captured at order-raise time which the doc gets right, but the matrix abstraction and currency dimension do not exist.
Spring Modulith @ApplicationModuleListener after-commit events (RadiologyOrderAccepted/RadiologyOrderCompleted): ABSENT. No events are emitted; lifecycle methods directly mutate and save the entity.

@PreAuthorize privilege gating ('RADIOLOGY-*' / part of the 177-code set) on radiology endpoints: ABSENT/inactive. Every radiology lifecycle endpoint in PatientResource.java has its @PreAuthorize commented out (e.g. line 1975, 6075); the only active @PreAuthorize in the file are PATIENT-* on registration endpoints (lines 289-681). So radiology accept/reject/hold/verify/results/report/attachments are effectively unauthorized at method level in legacy.

Hibernate Envers audit baseline (@Audited) on Radiology or any entity: ABSENT (0 @Audited across com.orbix.api). Device-fingerprint/device-binding: ABSENT (0 references). Both are bundled-but-unimplemented phantom features.

verification.summary: Verdict: ACCURATE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. I opened every cited file and spot-checked every load-bearing line range. The extraction is of high quality: the workflow state machine (PatientResource.java:4200-4335), order-creation guards (1974-2016 + PatientServiceImpl.java:991-1062), billing math including the INSURANCE-covered vs ADMISSION-uninsured branches (1063-1225), delete cleanup with the verified 'j = j++' no-op bug (3455) and the missing isPresent() check before r.get() at 3423, add_report bill-status gate (3191), save_reason_for_rejection (2026), attachment guards and filename construction (2922-2996, 6143-6157), the LAB-vs-RADIOLOGY delete-guard miscitation finding (lab at 6021-6022, radiology at 6154-6156), the encounter uncleared-bill blockers (5477, 5646), and the cleanup deletes (453-468) ALL verified exactly as stated, including verbatim exception messages and the legacy copy-paste 'accept' verbs in reject/collect messages. Entity facts verified: Radiology.report length 10000 (Radiology.java:47-48), held/collected columns (127-137), RadiologyType.price double (RadiologyType.java:50), RadiologyTypeInsurancePlan price/covered double (42,44) and table name at 36, RadiologyAttachment table 'radiology_attachments' (RadiologyAttachment.java:27). ALL phantom-feature/ABSENCE claims independently confirmed: @Audited = 0 occurrences across com.orbix.api; fingerprint/deviceId/device_id/deviceBinding = 0 occurrences; radiology lifecycle endpoints have @PreAuthorize commented (1975, 6075) or entirely absent (4200-4335) while active PATIENT-* auth exists only on registration endpoints (289, 379, 399, 509, 548, 606, 681); no COMPLETED/CANCELLED/SCHEDULED/HELD status; no RadiologyReport entity; no ClinicalOrder aggregate; no Modulith events; no document/batch numbering for radiology (identity is DB IDENTITY id only). Supporting artefacts confirmed: RadiologyTypeInsurancePlanRepository.findByRadiologyTypeAndInsurancePlanAndCovered (line 43) and PatientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo() exist. The only defects are minor precision/anchoring issues — none change behaviour: (1) the worklist param is 'id' not 'patient_no'; (2) an inline '4119' anchor for the outsider list is wrong (correct is 3719); (3) the three raw worklists have differing payment-status filters ({PAID,COVERED} for outsider/outpatient vs {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED} for inpatient) that the extraction did not enumerate; (4) the two publicPath error-message variants are not disambiguated. I could not exhaustively prove the universal 'COLLECTED is never consumed' negative across all 6455 lines, but it is strongly supported by every read path I inspected. The extraction's HIGH confidence is justified.

extraction.context:
LEGACY PROCEDURE + THEATRE behaviour extraction for inc-06. Source of truth: D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature/Zana-HMIS-API/api/api/src/main/java/com/orbix/api. Procedure ordering/accept/note/delete lifecycle lives entirely inside PatientResource.java + PatientServiceImpl.java. Master data: ProcedureType (price), ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan (per-plan price/covered), Theatre (pure master-data). Frontend: doctor-cracking.component.ts is the real order+note screen.

EARLY-DISCOVERY FINDINGS (both confirmed by scan for this context):
1. AUDIT TRAIL: No Hibernate Envers audit trail is effectively active. Procedure.java, ProcedureType.java, ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan.java, Theatre.java carry NO @Audited annotation (verified by reading all four entities in full). The entities track actor/day stamps manually via plain columns only: createdBy/createdOn/createdAt, orderedby/orderedOn/orderedAt, acceptedby/acceptedOn/acceptedAt, heldby/heldOn/heldAt, rejectedby/rejectedOn/rejectedAt + rejectComment, verifiedby/verifiedOn/verifiedAt (Procedure.java:110-145). Downstream agents must not assume an Envers audit baseline exists.
2. DEVICE-FINGERPRINT / DEVICE-BINDING: No device-fingerprint or device-binding feature exists in any procedure/theatre code path or its security annotations. Procedure lifecycle endpoints have NO @PreAuthorize at all; only master-data save endpoints use role-string @PreAuthorize('hasAnyAuthority(ADMIN-ACCESS)'). Agents must not treat device-binding as a feature to preserve.

OVERALL: Legacy procedure lifecycle has exactly 3 reachable statuses (PENDING -> ACCEPTED, and PENDING/PAID -> VERIFIED). There is NO theatre-scheduling, NO operative record, NO surgeon role/approval, NO APPROVED/REJECTED/HELD/COLLECTED status reachable for a Procedure. Theatre is only a master-data tag (model.setTheatre).

CONFIDENCE: HIGH on backend (read every relevant method + entity verbatim). The Angular patient-procedure.component.ts calls 4 endpoints that DO NOT EXIST in the backend (dead UI) -- documented under absentInLegacy.
