capabilities.description: If procedure.type equals 'THEATRE', resolve Theatre by NAME; if not found throw 'Theatre not found'; else setTheatre(found). If type != 'THEATRE', setTheatre(null) (PatientServiceImpl.java:1233-1242). DiagnosisType: if diagnosisType.id != null resolve by id (throw 'Diagnosis Type not found' if absent) else setDiagnosisType(null) (:1243-1252). ProcedureType must exist else 'Procedure type not found' (:1254-1256). Exactly ONE of consultation/nonConsultation/admission must be present: every other combination throws 'Could not save, procedure should not have more than two properties' (:1257-1268) and the all-absent case throws 'Could not save, procedure should have one property' (:1269-1271). Consultation path sets clinician from consultation (:1272-1276). NonConsultation path: status PENDING->IN-PROCESS (saved), IN-PROCESS reused, else throw 'Could not be done. Patient already signed off' (:1278-1290). Admission path: status PENDING throws 'Could not be done. Admission not verified', IN-PROCESS reused, else 'Could not be done. Patient already signed off'; clinician resolved from current user (:1292-1307). procedure.status set to 'PENDING' (:1313).

capabilities.name: BILLING MATH on order: cash vs insurance-covered vs admission-uncovered
capabilities.description: A PatientBill is always created first: amount=procedureType.price, paid=0, balance=price, qty=1, billItem='Procedure', description='Procedure: '+procedureType.name, status='UNPAID' (PatientServiceImpl.java:1314-1326). Branch taken only if patient.paymentType=='INSURANCE' OR admission present (:1328). INSURANCE-COVERED: lookup ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan by (procedureType, patient.insurancePlan, covered=true); if present override bill: amount=plan.price, paid=plan.price, balance=0, status='COVERED', paymentType='INSURANCE', membershipNo=patient.membershipNo, insurancePlan=plan.insurancePlan (:1330-1340), then attach to a PENDING PatientInvoice (create one if none; PatientInvoiceDetail amount=bill.amount, qty=1, description='Procedure: '+name) (:1342-1402). ADMISSION-NOT-COVERED (plan absent but admission present): bill amount=price, paid=0, balance=price, status='VERIFIED' (:1403-1409), attached to a PENDING invoice whose insurancePlan is NULL (:1411-1471). CASH outpatient (no insurance, no admission): bill stays UNPAID at price, no invoice. Finally procedure.patient + procedure.patientBill set and saved (:1474-1476). NOTE legacy quirk: invoice.no is first set to String.valueOf(Math.random()) then overwritten with the generated id string (:1348-1369, :1417-1438).

capabilities.name: ACCEPT procedure endpoint + guard (PENDING/REJECTED -> ACCEPTED)
capabilities.description: POST /patients/accept_procedure (no @PreAuthorize), body LProcedure{id,note}. Procedure must exist else 'Procedure not found'. Guard: status must be 'PENDING' OR 'REJECTED' else throws 'Could not accept, only PENDING or REJECTED tests can be accepted' (note the verbatim word 'tests'). Sets acceptedby/On/At = current; clears rejectedby/On/At=null and rejectComment=''; sets status='ACCEPTED'; saves. Returns true. NOTE: legacy never sets status to REJECTED for a Procedure anywhere, so the REJECTED branch of this guard is effectively unreachable in normal flow.

capabilities.name: UPDATE procedure note endpoint + guard (ACCEPTED only)
capabilities.description: POST /patients/update_procedure (no @PreAuthorize), body LProcedure{id,note}. Procedure must exist else 'Procedure not found'. Guard: status must equal 'ACCEPTED' else 'Could not update, only accepted procedure can be updated'. Sets note=pro.note; saves; returns true. Does NOT change status.

capabilities.name: ADD NOTE endpoint = the real VERIFY path + BILL GATE (PENDING -> VERIFIED)
capabilities.description: POST /patients/procedures/add_note (no @PreAuthorize), body Procedure{id,note}. Procedure must exist else 'Procedure not found' (PatientResource.java:3402-3404). Empty note rejected: 'Could not add empty procedure note' (:3405-3407). BILL GATE: only proceeds if the procedure's PatientBill.status is one of 'PAID','COVERED','VERIFIED' (:3408); then sets note, sets procedure.status='VERIFIED', saves (:3409-3412). Otherwise throws 'Could not add procedure note. Payment not verified' (:3413-3414). This is the ONLY code path that sets a Procedure to VERIFIED.

capabilities.name: DELETE procedure endpoint + guard (PENDING only) and credit-note path
capabilities.description: POST /patients/delete_procedure (no @PreAuthorize), param id. Guard: status must equal 'PENDING' else 'Could not delete, only a PENDING procedure can be deleted' (PatientResource.java:3478-3479). Loads the PatientBill; if a PatientPaymentDetail exists with status 'RECEIVED', creates a PatientCreditNote (amount=bill.amount, reference='Canceled procedure', status='PENDING', no=patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo().getNo()) (:3485-3511). Deletes the associated PatientInvoiceDetail if present, and deletes the parent PatientInvoice if it has zero details (NOTE bug: counter uses j=j++ so j stays 0, so parent invoice is ALWAYS deleted) (:3513-3527). Deletes PatientPaymentDetail if present (:3528-3532). Deletes the Procedure then the PatientBill (:3534-3535). Returns true.

capabilities.name: BILL GATE detail: cashier payment turns UNPAID/VERIFIED bill -> PAID
capabilities.description: The procedure bill (billItem 'Procedure') is paid by the generic cashier flow, not a procedure-specific endpoint. Each bill must be 'UNPAID' or 'VERIFIED' else throws 'One or more bills have been paid/covered/canceled. Only unpaid or verified bills can be paid' (PatientBillResource.java:295-296). On pay: balance=0, paid=amount, status='PAID' (:304-307); a PatientPaymentDetail(status RECEIVED) and a Collection (paymentChannel 'Cash', paymentReferenceNo 'NA') are created (:310-337); any PatientInvoiceDetail is marked 'PAID' and its invoice.amountPaid incremented (:341-350). After bill is PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED the doctor can call add_note to push the procedure to VERIFIED.

capabilities.name: Sign-off / patient-type-change GATES depend on procedure bill status
capabilities.description: On consultation sign-out and on patient-type change, any procedure whose PatientBill.status == 'UNPAID' blocks the operation: sign-off paths throw 'Could not save. Patient have uncleared procedure bill(s)' (PatientResource.java:5483-5489 and 5652-5658). Patient-type change: PENDING procedures with UNPAID bills are deleted with their bill; any non-UNPAID makes cancelable=false -> 'Can not change patient type, the has pending paid services. Please consider clearing with the patient.' (:472-490, :493-494).

capabilities.name: ProcedureType master data + pricing (cash price) and per-plan pricing
capabilities.description: ProcedureType: code(unique,@NotBlank), name(unique,@NotBlank), description, price(double,@NotNull), uom, active(default false) (ProcedureType.java:42-52). ProcedureTypeResource: GET /procedure_types, /get, /get_names, /load_procedure_types_like, /load_procedure_types_like_and_minor; POST /procedure_types/save requires @PreAuthorize('hasAnyAuthority(ADMIN-ACCESS)') (ProcedureTypeResource.java:77-78). Per-plan price: ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan{price(double,@NotNull), active(default false), covered(default false), insurancePlan FK, procedureType FK} (ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan.java:41-54). ProcedurePlanResource POST /procedure_type_insurance_plans/save requires ADMIN-ACCESS, forces active=true on create, duplicate (plan,type) guarded with 'Could not create plan, a similar plan already exist. Please consider ediiting the existing plan' (sic) (ProcedurePlanResource.java:72-101). Insurance billing keys off covered=true only (PatientServiceImpl.java:1330).

capabilities.name: NUMBERING SCHEMES touching procedures (no procedure number; invoice/credit-note schemes)
capabilities.description: A Procedure itself has NO business number (only DB IDENTITY id, Procedure.java:42). PatientInvoice.no generated by: set to String.valueOf(Math.random()) on first save, then overwritten with the entity id as string (PatientInvoice.no = id.toString()) (PatientServiceImpl.java:1348-1369 and 1417-1438) -- no prefix, no zero-padding, no fiscal-year reset. PatientCreditNote.no obtained from patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo().getNo() during procedure cancel (PatientResource.java:3505). Theatre/ProcedureType use a manually entered unique 'code', not auto-generated.

capabilities.name: REPORT queries touching procedures (procedure_report, procedure_collection_report, revenue)
capabilities.description: POST /reports/procedure_report: requires existing Clinician (else 'Clinician not found'); query procedureRepository.findAllByClinicianAndCreatedAtBetween(clinician, from.atStartOfDay(), to.atStartOfDay().plusDays(1)); for each procedure attaches insurancePlan from its PatientInvoiceDetail->PatientInvoice (null if none) (ReportResource.java:191-214). POST /reports/procedure_collection_report: gathers Collections in date range (optionally filtered by cashier nickname), maps their PatientBills, then findAllByPatientBillIn(bills); cashier name matched by collection.patientBill.id == procedure.patientBill.id (NOTE uses == on Long objects) (ReportResource.java:766-799). Revenue report sums procedure revenue as pro.getPatientBill().getAmount() (ReportResource.java:1259-1261). Date filter convention everywhere: from.atStartOfDay() inclusive to to.atStartOfDay().plusDays(1) exclusive.

capabilities.name: Insurance coverage advisory message shown by ordering UI
capabilities.description: After save_procedure the doctor-cracking UI inspects the returned bill: if patientBill.status != 'COVERED' AND consultation.paymentType === 'INSURANCE' it shows 'Service not covered in insurance package!'; otherwise 'Procedure Saved successifully'. This is purely advisory (the bill was still created as UNPAID/VERIFIED); no backend block.

capabilities.name: THEATRE vs NON-THEATRE selection at point of order (UI)
capabilities.description: doctor-cracking toggleTheatre() flips procedureType string between 'THEATRE' and 'NON-THEATRE'; when leaving THEATRE it clears theatre name/date/time/hours/minutes (doctor-cracking.component.ts:226-238). Theatre names loaded from GET /theatres/get_names (:723-741). save payload sends type=this.procedureType and theatre={name:this.theatreName} (:903-920). Backend only acts on the literal 'THEATRE' to resolve+attach a Theatre; any other type value nulls the theatre (PatientServiceImpl.java:1233-1242).

absentInLegacy: ABSENT in legacy: any APPROVED status for a Procedure. The procedure status only ever becomes PENDING (PatientServiceImpl.java:1313), ACCEPTED (PatientResource.java:4047), or VERIFIED (PatientResource.java:3410). All setStatus('APPROVED') occurrences in PatientResource (lines 5378, 5627, 5677 etc.) operate on Admission/Invoice/claim objects with setApprovedBy/On/At fields that Procedure does not have; procedures are never set to APPROVED.

ABSENT in legacy: any operative record / surgical note / operation log subsystem. The only procedure-level free-text is Procedure.note (max 10000 chars) set via add_note. Grep for operative/surgeon/surgery/Operative across com.orbix.api returns ZERO files.

ABSENT in legacy: any surgeon role, surgeon assignment, or surgeon approval/sign-off. There is no Surgeon entity/field; the clinician on a procedure is just the ordering consultation's clinician or the current user (PatientServiceImpl.java:1275, 1301-1303). No @PreAuthorize/role gate exists on any procedure lifecycle endpoint.

ABSENT in legacy: theatre scheduling / theatre slots / theatre booking calendar / theatre availability or conflict checks. Theatre entity has no time/slot/schedule fields (Theatre.java:29-46); a procedure merely references a theatre by name as a tag. date/time/hours/minutes on Procedure are free-form captured values with NO booking, NO collision detection, NO availability validation.

ABSENT in legacy: a working reject_procedure endpoint. doctor patient-procedure.component.ts:156 POSTs /patients/reject_procedure but NO such endpoint exists in the backend (grep across com.orbix.api returns nothing) -> this UI action 404s. A Procedure is therefore never actually set to REJECTED, making the REJECTED branch of accept_procedure's guard (PatientResource.java:4034) unreachable.

ABSENT in legacy: a working hold_procedure endpoint. patient-procedure.component.ts:178 POSTs /patients/hold_procedure; no backend endpoint exists. The HELD actor columns on Procedure (Procedure.java:128-132) are never written by any code path.

ABSENT in legacy: a working collect_procedure endpoint / any COLLECTED status. patient-procedure.component.ts:200 POSTs /patients/collect_procedure; no backend endpoint exists. 'COLLECTED' is never assigned to a Procedure anywhere.

ABSENT in legacy: a working verify_procedure endpoint. patient-procedure.component.ts:222 POSTs /patients/verify_procedure; no such backend endpoint exists. The ONLY verify mechanism is /patients/procedures/add_note which sets status VERIFIED (PatientResource.java:3410). Any inc-06 claim of a dedicated 'verify procedure' action must map onto add_note, not a separate verb.

ABSENT in legacy: Hibernate Envers @Audited on Procedure/ProcedureType/ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan/Theatre. No audit-history table exists for procedures; only manual actor/day/timestamp columns. Do not assume an Envers audit baseline.

ABSENT in legacy: any device-fingerprint / device-binding on procedure or theatre endpoints. Procedure lifecycle endpoints have NO security annotation at all; only master-data save endpoints use plain role strings (ADMIN-ACCESS).

ABSENT in legacy: a state machine richer than 3 states. There is no transition ACCEPTED->VERIFIED in code; VERIFIED is reached from add_note which only checks BILL status (PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED), independent of whether the procedure is PENDING or ACCEPTED. ACCEPTED is a side branch (accept_procedure) that update_procedure can edit but that does not gate verification. Do not invent a strict ordered PENDING->ACCEPTED->VERIFIED pipeline.

ABSENT in legacy: any deletion of a Theatre. TheatreServiceImpl.allowDeleteTheatre() unconditionally returns false, so deleteTheatre always throws 'Deleting this theatre is not allowed' (TheatreServiceImpl.java:78-91); there is also no delete endpoint wired in TheatreResource.

ABSENT in legacy: quantity / multi-unit procedure billing. Procedure bill is hard-coded qty=1 (PatientServiceImpl.java:1318); no per-procedure quantity, no co-payment split, no waiver logic for procedures (insurance is all-or-nothing via covered=true).

corrections: CITATION FORMAT (systemic, not content-wrong): Every citation uses a bare filename (e.g., 'Procedure.java:40-147', 'PatientServiceImpl.java:1314-1476') with NO package path. The actual files live under com/orbix/api/domain/ (entities), com/orbix/api/api/ (controllers, note the doubled 'api/api' segment), com/orbix/api/service/ (services), and com/orbix/api/repositories/. Two distinct files even share a base name where only the path disambiguates them. A downstream developer cannot grep-resolve some of these unambiguously from the citation alone. Recommend every citation be re-emitted with its full path (verified examples: D:/.../com/orbix/api/domain/Procedure.java:54 for status; D:/.../com/orbix/api/service/PatientServiceImpl.java:1233-1242 for theatre resolution; D:/.../com/orbix/api/api/PatientResource.java:3397-3416 for add_note). All line numbers themselves were checked and are CORRECT.

OMISSION (ProcedurePlanResource): The extraction lists only 'GET ... POST /save' for ProcedurePlanResource and does not mention the live POST /procedure_type_insurance_plans/delete endpoint at ProcedurePlanResource.java:104-117, which carries @PreAuthorize('hasAnyAuthority(ADMIN-ACCESS)') and actually deletes a plan (procedureTypeInsurancePlanRepository.delete at :114). This is a real, reachable mutation endpoint and a notable contrast to Theatre (which cannot be deleted). The cited range '59-117' technically spans it, but the capability text omits its existence.

IMPRECISION (ProcedurePlanResource duplicate guard is DEAD CODE): The extraction states the duplicate (plan,type) is 'guarded with' the message at ProcedurePlanResource.java:88 as if it is a live guard. In fact the duplicate check at :87-89 is nested inside 'if(procedureType.getId() == null)' at :86, and procedureType is obtained via procedureTypeRepository.findByName(...).get() at :81 — a persisted entity whose id is never null. Therefore the duplicate guard is UNREACHABLE/dead code. Given the extraction's own diligence in flagging dead/no-op code elsewhere (j=j++, Math.random), this guard should likewise be flagged as effectively inert, not described as an active duplicate protection.

IMPRECISION (doctor-cracking.component.ts toggleTheatre): The extraction says leaving THEATRE 'clears theatre name/date/time/hours/minutes' (doctor-cracking.component.ts:226-238). Verified: lines 233-234 are 'this.procedureDate!' and 'this.procedureTime!' — TypeScript non-null-assertion expression statements that are NO-OPS; they do NOT clear/reset date or time. Only procedureTheatreName (:232), procedureHours (:235) and procedureMinutes (:236) are actually reset. So 'clears ... date/time' is inaccurate; date and time are not cleared. (Tangential observation: the field reset on toggle is 'procedureTheatreName' :232 while the save payload sends 'theatre={name:this.theatreName}' :910 — a different variable — a latent frontend bug, though the extraction's payload citation itself is correct.)

MINOR INCOMPLETENESS (revenue report): The extraction says 'Revenue report sums procedure revenue as pro.getPatientBill().getAmount() (ReportResource.java:1259-1261)'. The formula is correct, but line 1260 shows the sum is gated by 'if(pro.getPatientBill().getInsurancePlan() == r.getInsurancePlan())' — a per-insurance-plan grouping condition that ALSO uses Java reference-equality (==) on objects, the same autoboxing/identity hazard the extraction flagged for the collection report. This grouping key and the == hazard on the revenue path are not mentioned.
unverifiableClaims: Whether the Angular patient-procedure.component.ts actions truly '404' at runtime: I confirmed (a) the four endpoints (reject/hold/collect/verify_procedure) are POSTed by the frontend at patient-procedure.component.ts:156/178/200/222 and (b) a backend grep across com.orbix.api returns ZERO matches for those four paths. The conclusion that the calls fail is a sound inference, but the literal HTTP 404 status (vs. another error) is a runtime behaviour not directly evidenced in static source; the static facts behind it are fully verified.

The blanket absence assertions scoped to 'across com.orbix.api' (e.g., zero operative/surgeon/surgery files, no @Audited anywhere, no reject/hold/collect/verify_procedure endpoints) were each re-run by me with Grep over the exact com/orbix/api root and returned zero matches, so they are verified for that tree. They remain 'unverifiable' only in the narrow sense that they cannot be guaranteed for any code outside that directory (none is expected, but it was not exhaustively scanned beyond this root and the two named Angular components).
verification.summary:
I adversarially verified the 'Legacy Procedure + Theatre' extraction by opening every cited file at the cited lines. The legacy tree is at D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature/Zana-HMIS-API/api/api/src/main/java/com/orbix/api with entities under /domain, controllers under /api, services under /service. CORE FINDING: the extraction is substantively ACCURATE and unusually high-fidelity. Every behavioural claim I checked matched source exactly, and every absence claim is confirmed.

VERIFIED CORRECT (line numbers exact): Procedure entity fields incl. plain-String status (Procedure.java:54), nullable theatre FK (:59-62), mandatory procedureType/patientBill/patient, manual actor stamps :110-145, note length 10000 :44; Theatre is pure master-data with no schedule/slot/surgeon fields (Theatre.java:29-46); saveProcedure logic incl. THEATRE-by-name resolution (:1233-1242), context exclusivity guards (:1257-1271), status PENDING (:1313), full billing math for cash/insurance-COVERED/admission-VERIFIED branches (:1314-1476) including the Math.random()-then-id quirk; accept_procedure PENDING/REJECTED->ACCEPTED with verbatim 'tests' wording (PatientResource.java:4026-4050); update_procedure ACCEPTED-only (:4052-4067); add_note as the ONLY VERIFIED path with bill-gate PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED (:3397-3416); delete_procedure PENDING-only + credit-note + the j=j++ no-op bug (:3473-3537); cashier pay flow (PatientBillResource.java:290-350); sign-off and patient-type-change gates (:472-494, :5483-5489, :5652-5658); ProcedureType/ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan/ProcedureTypeResource master-data + ADMIN-ACCESS @PreAuthorize; all three report queries incl. the == on Long bug in procedure_collection_report (:766-799).

ABSENCE CLAIMS CONFIRMED via my own greps (all zero matches): no reject/hold/collect/verify_procedure backend endpoints (so the 4 frontend calls at patient-procedure.component.ts:156/178/200/222 are dead UI); no @Audited anywhere (no Envers baseline); no operative/surgeon/surgery code; no device-fingerprint/binding on procedure paths. Cross-checked setStatus calls: Procedure is set ONLY to PENDING/ACCEPTED/VERIFIED — every APPROVED/REJECTED/HELD/COLLECTED setStatus operates on LabTest/Radiology/Admission/Invoice objects, never Procedure. The 3-state machine claim holds.

The corrections are limited to: (1) a systemic citation-format weakness (bare filenames, no package paths — line numbers are right but paths are needed to disambiguate), (2) an omitted live ADMIN-ACCESS delete endpoint in ProcedurePlanResource (:104-117), (3) the ProcedurePlanResource duplicate guard being unreachable dead code (nested under an always-false id==null check at :86) yet described as an active guard, (4) toggleTheatre not actually clearing date/time (non-null-assertion no-ops at :233-234), and (5) the revenue report's omitted per-plan grouping key plus its own == reference-equality hazard. None of these undermines the extraction's conclusions; they sharpen precision and completeness for downstream implementers.

extractions.context: Legacy department-side EXTRAS not in OPD ordering

extraction.context: Legacy DEPARTMENT-SIDE behaviour for lab, radiology, and procedure that is distinct from the inc-05 doctor OPD ORDER flow. All lab/rad/proc lifecycle lives in ONE controller, com.orbix.api.api.PatientResource.java; entities under com/orbix/api/domain. Legacy root D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature/Zana-HMIS-API/api/api/src/main/java/com/orbix/api. AUDIT FINDING: no Hibernate Envers active, no @Audited on LabTest, Radiology, Procedure, LabTestTypeRange, or any scanned entity; audit equals the in-row actor-stamp columns (created/ordered/accepted/held/rejected/collected/verified _by/_on/_at) shown as 'timestamp | nickname' strings (PatientResource.java:3850-3880). DEVICE-BINDING FINDING: none in any lab/rad/proc path or Angular department component. DEPARTMENT ENTRY GATE: orders surface only when PatientBill.status is PAID or COVERED (outpatient/outsider) or PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED (inpatient and report-add); this is a runtime worklist FILTER, NOT a settled flag and NOT a transition precondition (PatientResource.java:3676,3693,3710,3727,3744,3761,3778,3795,3820,3389,3408,3191). Flow is per-patient: pick a patient from a list, then act on that patient's tests one at a time; there is no department-wide pooled worklist. VERBATIM messages (lines): 'Could not verify, only COLLECTED tests can be verified' (3969); 'Could not hold, only ACCEPTED tests can be held' (4014); 'Could not verify, only ACCEPTED radiologies can be verified' (4281); 'Could not update, only accepted procedure can be updated' (4061); 'Could not add empty procedure note' (3406); 'Could not add report. Payment not verified' (3393/3195); 'Could not delete. Lab Test already verified' (6022); 'Could not delete. Radiology already verified' (6155). Angular toasts: 'Status changed : ACCEPTED/REJECTED/PENDING/COLLECTED' and 'VERIFIED, Saved successifully' (sic) (lab-test.component.ts:150-290). Confidence basis: every endpoint, guard, and status literal read directly; absence claims verified by repo-wide grep returning zero matches.

capabilities.name: Worklists per-patient and payment-filtered, not a pooled settled queue
capabilities.description: Three lab list endpoints return PATIENTS de-duped via HashSet: get_lab_outpatient_list (LabTests on Consultation IN-PROCESS, type OUTPATIENT, bill PAID or COVERED), get_lab_inpatient_list (Admission IN-PROCESS, INPATIENT, bill PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED), get_lab_outsider_list (NonConsultation IN-PROCESS, OUTSIDER, bill PAID or COVERED). get_lab_tests_by_patient_id returns that patient's tests filtered to bill PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED, each lifecycle field shown as timestamp pipe nickname. Radiology mirrors; procedure has outpatient and outsider only with no inpatient list, and both procedure lists build the Location URI with the wrong lab path. No settledOnly param, no patientClass enum.

capabilities.name: Lab lifecycle PENDING ACCEPTED COLLECTED VERIFIED with REJECTED branch and HOLD that reverts to PENDING; results free-text with no flag
capabilities.description: accept requires PENDING or REJECTED (clears rejected stamps); reject requires PENDING or ACCEPTED (clears accepted stamps); collect requires ACCEPTED; verify requires COLLECTED and copies result/level/range/unit; hold requires ACCEPTED, stamps held, and sets status back to PENDING (no HELD state, no COMPLETED state). save_lab_test_results requires COLLECTED. save_reason_for_rejection edits rejectComment only when REJECTED. Results are four free-text strings; LabTestTypeRange is a named picklist with only id name labTestType (no numeric thresholds, sex, age, analyte, or flag); a LabTest holds exactly one result string.

capabilities.name: Report gated on payment not status; radiology verify gated ACCEPTED with orphan collect111; procedure ends VERIFIED via add_note
capabilities.description: lab_tests add_report and radiologies add_report write the report column gated only on bill PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED and never change lifecycle status. Radiology: accept PENDING/REJECTED, reject PENDING/ACCEPTED, hold ACCEPTED->PENDING; verify_radiology requires ACCEPTED not COLLECTED (diverges from lab) and copies result plus attachment bytes; save requires ACCEPTED; collect_radiology111 (literal 111 in path) requires ACCEPTED and sets COLLECTED but strands the row. Procedure: only accept_procedure (PENDING/REJECTED to ACCEPTED), update_procedure (requires ACCEPTED, edits note), procedures add_note (non-empty note plus bill PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED, sets note and VERIFIED). No procedure reject/hold/collect or standalone verify despite rejected/held columns; no collected columns.

capabilities.name: Attachments local-filesystem streaming with delete guard VERIFIED; reports in-memory date-range with no aggregation; no document numbers; no price recompute
capabilities.description: Upload persists rows in lab_test_attachment and radiology_attachment per-type tables; download reads bytes from CompanyProfile.publicPath via Files.readAllBytes inline; delete blocks only when the owning LabTest or Radiology is VERIFIED; Procedure has no attachments. get_lab_tests_by_date and lab_test_statistics_report return VERIFIED rows; lab_sample_collection_report returns all statuses; collection reports attach cashier and dateTime per cashier Collection row; windows half-open start-of-day to to-plus-one-day; no grouping SQL, no scheduled job, no server PDF. LabTest/Radiology/Procedure use IDENTITY Long id with no accession/batch/document number; no transition reads or writes any amount.

absentInLegacy: LabBatch or batch grouping by lab test type plus order date - ABSENT. No LabBatch entity, no lab_batch/lab_batch_member table, no batch accept/complete; lab worklist is per-patient one test at a time.
seq_lab_batch_no sequence and a LABB-date-seq document number - ABSENT. Identity is the IDENTITY Long id only.
OperativeRecord aggregate and required-before-complete HTTP 422 - ABSENT. The procedure narrative is the free-text Procedure.note written by add_note which sets VERIFIED.

Procedure APPROVED state and a surgeon/anaesthetist approve endpoint with PROCEDURE_APPROVE privilege and self-approve config - ABSENT. Legacy flow is PENDING to ACCEPTED to VERIFIED; the M14 missing-APPROVED claim is an invention.
Theatre scheduling (schedule endpoint, theatreUid, scheduledAt, scheduledByUsername) - ABSENT. Theatre is only master-data set as Procedure.theatre at order time; no double-booking concept.

Server-side lab flag computation (ReferenceRange enum, analyte/sex/age resolution, value-below-critical logic) - ABSENT. LabTestTypeRange has only a free-text name; result/range/level/unit are free-text stored verbatim; the golden-master flag test targets nonexistent behaviour.
Per-analyte multi-line results (analyte master plus result-line entity) - ABSENT. A LabTest stores exactly one result string.
RadiologyReport as a distinct aggregate after image upload - ABSENT as a separate entity. Free-text lives in Radiology.report and Radiology.result columns; verify is gated ACCEPTED not after-images.

settled boolean plus SettlementDispatcher plus a CASH gate as a hard precondition returning HTTP 409 ORDER_NOT_SETTLED - ABSENT. No settled flag and no COMPLETED transition; the payment check is a worklist FILTER applied to queries and add_report only.
COMPLETED and CANCELLED statuses - ABSENT. Terminal state is VERIFIED; deletion is via separate credit-note endpoints; the PENDING-to-COMPLETED-forbidden guard targets a nonexistent transition.

EARLY-DISCOVERY FINDINGS (mandatory in every audit/auth-touching spec): (1) AUDIT TRAIL — No Hibernate Envers audit trail is effectively active in the legacy system; none of the L/R/P/Theatre type, range, or *InsurancePlan entities carries @Audited (LabTestType.java, LabTestTypeRange.java, RadiologyType.java, ProcedureType.java, Theatre.java, LabTestTypeInsurancePlan.java, RadiologyTypeInsurancePlan.java, ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan.java all have only @Entity/@Data/@Table). Downstream agents must not assume an Envers audit baseline exists. Per-row provenance is hand-rolled via createdBy/createdOn/createdAt (user-id + Day-id + timestamp) columns only. (2) DEVICE-BINDING — No device-fingerprint or device-binding feature exists anywhere in these master-data/pricing resources or services. Agents must not treat this as a feature to preserve.

WHAT INC-02 ALREADY COVERS (confirmed from 02 build spec §1.3, §1.4, §2): the five type master-data entities (LabTestType, LabTestTypeRange, RadiologyType, ProcedureType, Theatre) are scoped into inc-02 masterdata as legacy-faithful flat tables; and the seven legacy *InsurancePlan per-plan price tables are CONSOLIDATED into one unified ServicePrice matrix + PriceLookup.resolve() storage primitive [GATED:CR-04]. CRITICAL HAND-OFF: inc-02 spec §2.2/§2.3 EXPLICITLY defers the legacy resolve-TIME billing logic (two-step cash-then-covered override, per-service not-covered fallback asymmetry, ward top-up) to the BILLING increment (inc-04) code — PriceLookup.resolve() is only a row lookup. The point-of-care pricing math for L/R/P documented below (PatientServiceImpl) is therefore NOT in inc-02 and is the per-plan pricing nuance that must be reproduced in inc-04/inc-06 consuming code, NOT re-built as master-data.

capabilities.name: Type master-data CRUD — LabTestType / RadiologyType / ProcedureType (identical shape)
capabilities.description: Each is a flat catalog entity with @NotBlank unique code, @NotBlank unique name, description, @NotNull double price, uom (String), boolean active default false. GENERATED IDENTITY id. No status field. Save endpoint POST /lab_test_types/save | /radiology_types/save | /procedure_types/save, all gated @PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('ADMIN-ACCESS')"). On CREATE (id==null): service sets active=TRUE, createdBy=user id, createdOn=Day id, createdAt=timestamp. SANITIZATION differs by type: RadiologyType/ProcedureType save() run code=code.replace(" ","") and name=name.trim().replaceAll("\\s+"," ") on every save (RadiologyTypeServiceImpl.java:41-42, ProcedureTypeServiceImpl.java:41-42). LabTestType is the OUTLIER: sanitization (code.replace + name trim) runs ONLY on the UPDATE branch (id!=null) and is applied to the persisted copy's existing values, then immediately overwritten by testType.setName(labTestType.getName()) at line 62 — so on update only description/price/uom are actually persisted from the payload and CODE IS EFFECTIVELY IMMUTABLE on update (the update branch never copies code/name from the incoming payload). New LabTestType create branch does NO sanitization. deleteLabTestType/deleteRadiologyType/deleteProcedureType ALWAYS throw — allowDelete*() hard-returns false; verbatim message "Deleting this <type> is not allowed".

capabilities.name: Alternate pricing-management surface (InsurancePlanResource) — auto-create-on-read + coverage/price mutations
capabilities.description: GET /insurance_plans/get_lab_test_type_prices | get_radiology_type_prices | get_procedure_type_prices?insurance_plan_id=N returns one price row per type. If insurance_plan_id==0 -> returns the catalog cash price (type.getPrice()). If >0 -> for each type, if no *TypeInsurancePlan row exists it SILENTLY CREATES one (active=true, covered=false, price=0, provenance stamped) and persists it (SIDE-EFFECTING GET). insurance_plan_id<0 -> InvalidOperationException("Invalid package type selected"). change_*_type_coverage: to set covered=true the existing row price must be >0 else InvalidOperationException("Could not change coverage. Invalid price value. Should not be equal or less than zero"). update_*_type_price_by_insurance: planId==0 path updates the CATALOG cash price (price<0 -> "Invalid price value. Price should not be less than zero"); planId>0 path sets plan price, and price==0 auto-sets covered=false, price<0 -> "Invalid Price value. Price should not be less than zero". These endpoints carry NO @PreAuthorize (ungated — inc-02 DEVIATION-4/CR-15 flags tightening to ADMIN-ACCESS).

capabilities.name: POINT-OF-CARE pricing/billing math for L/R/P (the per-plan nuance NOT built in inc-02)
capabilities.description: On order creation (PatientServiceImpl.saveLabTest/saveRadiology/saveProcedure): a PatientBill is ALWAYS built at CASH first: amount=type.getPrice(), paid=0, balance=type.getPrice(), qty=1, billItem="Lab Test" (resp. radiology/procedure), description="Lab Test: "+typeName, status="UNPAID". THEN if patient.paymentType=="INSURANCE" OR an admission is present: look up the COVERED plan row via findBy<Type>AndInsurancePlanAndCovered(type, patient.getInsurancePlan(), true). IF a covered row exists -> override: amount=paid=planPrice, balance=0, status="COVERED", paymentType="INSURANCE", membershipNo set, insurancePlan set; then attach to a PENDING PatientInvoice for (patient, plan) — creating one if none (PatientInvoice.no first set to String.valueOf(Math.random()) then overwritten with the saved id.toString()), and add a PatientInvoiceDetail (amount=bill amount, qty=1). IF NO covered row exists AND admission present -> cash fallback: amount=type price, paid=0, balance=type price, status="VERIFIED" (inpatient), attached to a PENDING invoice with insurancePlan=null. IF NO covered row and NOT admitted -> the initial CASH UNPAID bill stays unchanged. The 'covered' flag (not 'active') is the decisive gate; price comes from the *TypeInsurancePlan.price when covered, else from the catalog type price.
capabilities.citation: PatientServiceImpl.java:818-988 (lab full path; cash bill 821-835, covered override 837-911, inpatient cash-VERIFIED fallback 912-981); :990-... saveRadiology mirrors via findByRadiologyTypeAndInsurancePlanAndCovered at :1079; saveProcedure via findByProcedureTypeAndInsurancePlanAndCovered at :1330 and :2094

capabilities.name: Lab lifecycle state machine (PatientResource)
capabilities.description: Statuses: PENDING -> ACCEPTED -> COLLECTED -> VERIFIED, with REJECTED branch. Order-raise sets status="PENDING" (PatientServiceImpl.java:819). accept_lab_test: guard requires status in {PENDING, REJECTED} else InvalidOperationException("Could not accept, only PENDING or REJECTED tests can be accepted"); sets ACCEPTED, stamps acceptedBy/On/At, clears reject fields. reject_lab_test: guard {PENDING, ACCEPTED} else "Could not accept, only PENDING or ACCEPTED tests can be rejected"; sets REJECTED, clears accepted fields. collect_lab_test: guard {ACCEPTED} else "Could not accept, only ACCEPTED tests can be collected"; sets COLLECTED. verify_lab_test: guard {COLLECTED} else "Could not verify, only COLLECTED tests can be verified". LabTest entity also has held* and rejectComment columns. Result fields on LabTest: result, report(len 10000), range(col 'rrange'), level, unit (free text — NO analyte rows).

capabilities.name: Radiology lifecycle state machine
capabilities.description: Mirrors lab: PENDING -> ACCEPTED -> COLLECTED -> VERIFIED + REJECTED. setStatus calls: ACCEPTED at PatientResource.java:4221, REJECTED :4246, PENDING(reset) :4267, VERIFIED :4292, COLLECTED :4333. Radiology result stored as free-text result/report(len 10000)/description plus legacy Byte[] attachment column AND a RadiologyAttachment OneToMany list (orphanRemoval). There is NO separate radiologist-report entity — the report is the 'report' String column on Radiology.

capabilities.name: Procedure lifecycle state machine (NO surgeon-approval, NO operative record)
capabilities.description: Procedure statuses: PENDING -> ACCEPTED (+ REJECTED). accept_procedure guard requires {PENDING, REJECTED} else InvalidOperationException("Could not accept, only PENDING or REJECTED tests can be accepted"); sets ACCEPTED. update_procedure guard {ACCEPTED} else "Could not update, only accepted procedure can be updated" — only sets the free-text note. There is NO "APPROVED" clinical procedure state (the legacy setStatus("APPROVED") calls all belong to PatientInvoice/credit-note/referral approval, NOT procedures). corrections: FILE PATHS IN EVERY CITATION ARE WRONG (systemic). The extraction cites the legacy root as '.../com/orbix/api' and writes citations like 'LabTestType.java:44-71', 'PatientResource.java:3896', 'TheatreServiceImpl.java:40-55'. The actual tree splits into two sub-packages: entities live in '.../com/orbix/api/domain/' and REST controllers in '.../com/orbix/api/api/'. Correct paths are e.g. com/orbix/api/domain/LabTestType.java, com/orbix/api/api/PatientResource.java, com/orbix/api/service/LabTestTypeServiceImpl.java. The line numbers are correct/near-correct but a grep-to-verify against the literal cited paths will FAIL. This violates the firm's citation standard and must be fixed before hand-off.

RADIOLOGY LIFECYCLE IS MIS-STATED. The extraction asserts radiology mirrors lab as 'PENDING -> ACCEPTED -> COLLECTED -> VERIFIED' and the absence-item claims 'Legacy lab AND radiology both require ACCEPTED -> COLLECTED -> VERIFIED'. This is FALSE for radiology: verify_radiology guards on status.equals("ACCEPTED") at PatientResource.java:4280 (message "Could not verify, only ACCEPTED radiologies can be verified" :4281), so radiology verifies DIRECTLY from ACCEPTED, never requiring COLLECTED. The collect endpoint is mapped to a dead/typo path '/patients/collect_radiology111' (PatientResource.java:4317) and sets COLLECTED (:4333) but nothing consumes it. Real radiology lifecycle = PENDING -> ACCEPTED -> VERIFIED (REJECTED branch; HELD resets to PENDING at :4267). Lab, by contrast, genuinely requires COLLECTED before verify (verify_lab_test guards COLLECTED at PatientResource.java:3968). The setStatus line citations (4221/4246/4267/4292/4333) are themselves all exact.

DELETE-MESSAGE VERBATIM STRING IS WRONG FOR 3 OF 4 TYPES. The 'Type master-data CRUD' capability claims the verbatim message is 'Deleting this <type> is not allowed'. The actual strings use the camelCase Java class name, not a human type name: 'Deleting this labTestType is not allowed' (LabTestTypeServiceImpl.java:92), 'Deleting this radiologyType is not allowed' (RadiologyTypeServiceImpl.java:78), 'Deleting this procedureType is not allowed' (ProcedureTypeServiceImpl.java:78), 'Deleting this labTestTypeRange is not allowed' (LabTestTypeRangeServiceImpl.java:74). Only Theatre happens to match the generalized phrasing: 'Deleting this theatre is not allowed' (TheatreServiceImpl.java:79). qa-test-engineer asserting on exact strings would get false negatives.

setStatus("APPROVED") OWNERSHIP IS PARTLY MIS-ATTRIBUTED. The Procedure capability claims the APPROVED calls 'all belong to PatientInvoice/credit-note/referral approval'. Grep shows 9 calls in PatientResource.java: invoice.setStatus("APPROVED") at :5355, :5606, :5866, :5885 (PatientInvoice) and p.get()/n.get().setStatus("APPROVED") at :5378, :5627, :5677, :5904, :5923 (DischargePlan / DeceasedNote objects in discharge/sign-out/deceased flows). The headline conclusion holds (NO Procedure is ever set to APPROVED, so the 'no APPROVED procedure state' absence claim is CORRECT), but the enumeration 'credit-note/referral approval' is inaccurate — the non-invoice APPROVED targets are discharge-plan and deceased-note, not credit notes or referrals.

MINOR CITATION-RANGE DRIFT (non-fatal but should be tightened). Save/delete method ranges are each off by ~1-2 lines: LabTestTypeServiceImpl save is 40-68 (cited 41-68), delete 86-104 (cited 87-104); Radiology/ProcedureTypeServiceImpl save is 38-54 (cited 39-54), delete 72-90 (cited 73-90). LabTestTypeRangeServiceImpl cited ':53-55' points at getLabTestTypeRanges, not a behavioral line. LabTest result fields start at :47 not :46. The high-value exact citations (sanitization :41-42 in Radiology/Procedure, the unconditional provenance stamp at LabTestTypeRangeServiceImpl :44-46, the ProcedurePlanResource bug at :86, all InsurancePlanResource and PatientServiceImpl line numbers) are EXACT.
unverifiableClaims: The extraction's cross-references to the inc-02 build spec and inc-06 planning doc (e.g. 'inc-02 [GATED:CR-04]', 'DEVIATION-4/CR-15', 'inc-02 build spec confirms 35 distinct codes, 26 live', the '177 @PreAuthorize codes' attributed to inc-06) were not opened during this verification — only the legacy source was checked. The legacy-side facts those statements rest on (single ADMIN-ACCESS gating, ungated InsurancePlanResource mutations, ungated lifecycle endpoints) ARE verified; the target-doc claim numbers themselves are out of scope here.

The claim that LabTest's report field is 'len 10000' is verified, but the description that LabTestTypeRange.labTestType cascade behaves as 'cascade delete' is loosely stated: parent LabTestType.labTestTypeRanges has orphanRemoval=true (domain/LabTestType.java:60), while the child's FK carries @OnDelete(NO_ACTION) (domain/LabTestTypeRange.java:46). The net delete behavior depends on whether removal goes through the parent collection (orphanRemoval) vs a direct repository delete; not exercised here.

Radiology saveRadiology and saveProcedure 'mirror' the lab point-of-care path: the covered-lookup lines were directly verified (radiology findByRadiologyTypeAndInsurancePlanAndCovered at PatientServiceImpl.java:1079; procedure findByProcedureTypeAndInsurancePlanAndCovered at :1330 and :2094), but the full cash-bill/COVERED-override/inpatient-VERIFIED-fallback bodies for radiology and procedure were not read line-by-line as the lab path (818-988) was. They are asserted to mirror lab and the anchor lines match, but byte-identical equivalence of every branch is not individually confirmed.

The existence of a separate 'LabTestPlanPrice.java' entity in the domain package (observed during the file listing) was not investigated. The extraction scoped itself to the three *TypeInsurancePlan tables for L/R/P and did not mention LabTestPlanPrice; whether it is a dead/duplicate legacy table affecting L/R/P pricing is unverified.
verification.summary:
Adversarial verification of the L/R/P + Theatre type/pricing extraction against the legacy source. VERDICT: ACCURATE_WITH_CORRECTIONS — the substantive behavioral claims are overwhelmingly correct and several are impressively precise, but there are real defects that must be fixed before hand-off.

WHAT HELD UP (verified file:line): (1) The headline ProcedurePlanResource bug is REAL and exactly cited — the duplicate guard keys on procedureType.getId()==null instead of procedureTypePlan.getId()==null (api/ProcedurePlanResource.java:86), making the check dead; the Lab/Radiology versions correctly key on the plan DTO id (LabTestTypePlanResource.java:82, RadiologyPlanResource.java:81). (2) The 'ediiting' typo duplicate message is verbatim (all three plan resources). (3) The LabTestType sanitization OUTLIER (sanitize runs only on the update branch against the persisted copy's own values, then name is overwritten by payload at service line 62, leaving code effectively immutable on update; create branch does no sanitization) is correct. (4) InsurancePlanResource side-effecting GET auto-create (active=true/covered=false/price=0, provenance stamped, persisted) at lines 179-192/311-324/442-455, all coverage/price validation messages and the two distinct casings ('Invalid price value' vs 'Invalid Price value'), and the fact that ALL these mutation/read endpoints are UNGATED (no @PreAuthorize) — all verified exactly. (5) The PatientServiceImpl point-of-care math is exact down to PatientInvoice.no = String.valueOf(Math.random()) then overwritten with id.toString() (:857/:878), the COVERED override (:837-911), and the inpatient cash-VERIFIED fallback (:912-981); radiology/procedure covered lookups confirmed at :1079/:1330/:2094. (6) Lab & procedure lifecycle guards/messages exact. (7) Attachment delete guard blocks on status=="VERIFIED" with 'Could not delete. Lab Test already verified' (PatientResource.java:6021-6022), endpoint @PreAuthorize commented out — exact; the refutation of inc-06's wrong COMPLETED/ATTACHMENT_DELETE_LOCKED claim is correct. (8) Entity shapes (the three identical *TypeInsurancePlan tables with covered/active doubles, no DB unique constraint; LabTestTypeRange as bare named-string with NO numeric/sex/age range; free-text LabTest/Radiology result fields, report len 10000; Radiology inline Byte[] + RadiologyAttachment list) — all verified. (9) PHANTOM-FEATURE ABSENCE CLAIMS CONFIRMED: @Audited = 0 hits across com.orbix.api; device-fingerprint/binding = 0 hits; no OperativeRecord/LabBatch entity; no procedure APPROVED state (the 9 setStatus("APPROVED") calls are PatientInvoice + DischargePlan/DeceasedNote, never Procedure); 'RadiologyReport' appears only as a report-query endpoint/args DTO in ReportResource.java:467-468,1574, not as an aggregate entity.

WHAT NEEDS CORRECTION: (a) Every citation's file PATH is wrong — entities are under .../api/domain/ and controllers under .../api/api/, not bare .../api/; line numbers are fine but literal-path grep verification fails. (b) The radiology lifecycle is mis-stated: legacy radiology verifies directly from ACCEPTED (verify guard at :4280), so it is PENDING->ACCEPTED->VERIFIED, NOT the lab-style PENDING->ACCEPTED->COLLECTED->VERIFIED the extraction asserts; the COLLECTED step exists only via a dead typo endpoint collect_radiology111 (:4317) and is never required. (c) The generic delete message 'Deleting this <type> is not allowed' is only verbatim for Theatre; the other three use camelCase class names. (d) Minor save/delete method line-range drift of 1-2 lines. None of these undermine the core extraction; the pricing/billing math, the bug findings, the gating analysis, and all phantom-feature absence claims are sound.

extractions.context: Legacy build-order: what truly comes next

extraction.context:
SCOPE: Survey of legacy resources NOT yet built (Pharmacy, Inventory/Procurement, HR/Payroll, Assets, Reporting, Inpatient/Admission/Ward-nursing) to inform the NEXT real increment, PLUS the phantom-feature delta against the inc-06/07/08/09/10 planning docs. LEGACY ROOT: D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature/Zana-HMIS-API/api/api/src/main/java/com/orbix/api.

STANDING-OBLIGATION FINDINGS (verified this scan): (1) No Hibernate Envers audit trail is effectively active — zero @Audited annotations anywhere in com.orbix.api (Grep @Audited → no files). Dependency present, never used. (2) No device-fingerprint / device-binding feature exists — Grep fingerprint|deviceId|deviceBinding|userAgent|getRemoteAddr|X-Device → no files. Agents must not treat either as a feature to preserve.

RECOMMENDED NEXT REAL INCREMENT: Pharmacy + Inventory + Procurement (planning Inc-08) is the correct next slice — it is the largest already-coded legacy subsystem (10 dedicated transfer/procurement service classes + ~2 dispensing blocks in PatientResource + PharmacyResource), it is self-contained (depends only on already-built Billing/Master-data/Clinical-prescription), and Inpatient/Nursing (Inc-07) explicitly depends on it (ConsumableStockService) per build-plan.md:40,60. HR/Payroll, Assets, and Reporting are far smaller and can follow.

SCALE + DEPENDENCY one-liners (cite resource files):
- Pharmacy dispensing: lives INSIDE PatientResource.java (issueMedicine 3200-3293, giveMedicine 6212-6322) + PharmacyResource.java (346 lines, mostly CRUD + manual update_stock 199-231). Depends on Billing (bill must be PAID/COVERED at order time), Clinical (Prescription created in OPD). FEFO+negative-stock+stock-card all here.
- Inventory transfers (RO/TO/RN dance): 10 service impls — StoreToPharmacyTO/RN, PharmacyToStoreRO, PharmacyToPharmacyRO/TO/RN — orchestrated by InternalOrderResource.java (~1400 lines, 50 endpoints, the single hub for BOTH store and pharmacy transfers). Depends on Store + Pharmacy master data.
- Procurement: LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl (336 ln) + GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl (217 ln) + LocalPurchaseOrderResource + GoodsReceivedNoteResource. Depends on Supplier, SupplierItemPrice, Store, StoreItem.
- HR/Payroll: PayrollServiceImpl (412 ln) + PayrollResource + EmployeeResource. Self-contained; depends only on Employee master data. Net-salary math only — no tax/PAYE/NSSF engine.
- Assets: AssetResource.java (86 ln) — bare 4-endpoint CRUD (list/get/save/delete), NO state machine, NO depreciation, NO numbering. @PreAuthorize on save is copy-paste EMPLOYEE-ALL (AssetResource.java:60).
- Reporting: ReportResource.java (~18 endpoints) + only 2 real report-service impls in reports/service (InventoryReportServiceImpl, LocalPurchaseOrderReportService). The 'inline-repository-query' model — NOT 29 service classes. Depends on every prior context.
- Inpatient/Admission/Ward-nursing: lives INSIDE PatientResource.java + PatientServiceImpl.java (admit/ward-bed billing 1760-1890, discharge 5253-5390). Only PatientVital/PatientNursingCarePlan/PatientDressingChart nursing-chart entities exist in domain. Depends on Billing + Ward master data + Pharmacy(consumables). Partly touched in Inc-05 (DeceasedNote/ReferralPlan).

capabilities.name: LPO (Local Purchase Order) state machine — full
capabilities.description: States: PENDING (on save, LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl.java:84) → VERIFIED (verify, requires PENDING + non-empty details, :117-124) → APPROVED (approve, requires VERIFIED, :145-152) → SUBMITTED (submit, requires APPROVED, :173-180) → RECEIVED (set by GRN approve, GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.java:184). Branches: RETURNED (only from PENDING or VERIFIED, :197-201) and REJECTED (only from PENDING or VERIFIED, :218-222). NO 'DRAFT' state exists (planning doc invents it). Edit allowed only while PENDING (:92-93). saveDetail rejects duplicate items (:255-256), rejects item not on supplier price list 'Item not valid for this supplier' (:250), price snapshotted from SupplierItemPrice (:266).

capabilities.name: GRN (Goods Received Note) state machine + atomic stock credit
capabilities.description: States: PENDING only → APPROVED. NO VERIFIED/RETURNED/REJECTED/SUBMITTED states on the GRN itself (planning doc invents them). create(): one GRN per LPO enforced 'Can not create GRN by the given LPO. GRN with the given LPO already exist' (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.java:68,71); detail status seeded 'NOT VERIFIED' (:98). approve() guards status==PENDING 'Can not approve. Not a pending GRN' (:117-118) AND every detail must be 'VERIFIED' else 'All the items in the GRN must be verified before approving.' (:121-122). On approve (one @Transactional): StoreItem.stock += receivedQty (:128-132), StoreStockCard RECEIPT row written only if receivedQty>0 (:135-148), StoreItemBatch rows created per GRN batch (:153-163), Purchase rows recorded (:171-181), and the linked LPO flipped to RECEIVED (:184). Stock math on double.

capabilities.name: Store→Pharmacy transfer (RO/TO/RN) — states, FEFO, stock-card timing
capabilities.description: PharmacyToStoreRO: SUBMITTED → IN-PROCESS (on TO create, StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:86-88) → GOODS-ISSUED (on TO issue, :240) → COMPLETED (on RN approve_receipt, InternalOrderResource.java:731). StoreToPharmacyTO: PENDING → VERIFIED (:186) → APPROVED (:208) → GOODS-ISSUED (:230) → COMPLETED (InternalOrderResource.java:739). SOURCE store stock is DECREMENTED at TO issue() (GOODS-ISSUED), NOT at RN: guard 'Can not issue goods. Stock qty is less than transfer qty at <item>' (:253-254), store stock card written there (:262-274). DESTINATION pharmacy stock CREDITED at RN approveReceivingNote (StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:206-241) — PharmacyMedicine.stock += receivedPharmacySKUQty, pharmacy stock card written if qty>0 (:214-228). RN approve_receipt guards status==PENDING 'Could not receive. Not a pending GRN' (InternalOrderResource.java:725-726). FEFO (getEarlierBatch): pick earliest expiry; if NO batch has expiry, pick lowest id (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:312-350). Coefficient applied at TO saveDetail: transferedPharmacySKUQty = transferedStoreSKUQty * coefficient (:424); missing coefficient → 'Item to medicine conversion factor does not exist...' (:419).

capabilities.name: Document numbering scheme — ALL not-yet-built docs
capabilities.description: Every document number is Formater.formatWithCurrentDate(PREFIX, id) = PREFIX + yyyyMMdd(LocalDateTime.now(), server default TZ) + '-' + id, where id = repository.getLastId()+1 (global table MAX(id)+1, race-prone, NO zero-padding, NO per-day/fiscal reset). Exact legacy prefixes: GRN=GRN (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.java:213), LPO=LPO (LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl.java:332), Payroll=PRL (PayrollServiceImpl.java:406), PatientCreditNote=PCN (PatientCreditNoteServiceImpl.java:39), PharmacyToStoreRO=PSR (PharmacyToStoreROServiceImpl.java:374), StoreToPharmacyTO=SPT (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:438), StoreToPharmacyRN=PGRN (StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:339), PharmacyToPharmacyRO=PPR (PharmacyToPharmacyROServiceImpl.java:379), PharmacyToPharmacyTO=SPT (PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:428 — SAME 'SPT' as Store→Pharmacy TO = real legacy prefix COLLISION), PharmacyToPharmacyRN=PPRN (PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:328). Format string in Formater.java:14-16.
capabilities.citation: Formater.java:14-16; GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.java:213; LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl.java:332; PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:428; StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:438; StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:339; PharmacyToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:328

capabilities.name: Pharmacy prescription dispensing (issueMedicine) — states, guards, FEFO, negative-stock
capabilities.description: Prescription dispensing state machine is TWO states only: NOT-GIVEN → GIVEN (PatientResource.java:3217,3230). NOT the planning doc's six-state PENDING/ACCEPTED/HELD/VERIFIED/APPROVED/SOLD. Guards: status must be NOT-GIVEN else 'Could not issue medicine. Prescription with id <id> is not a pending prescription' (:3217-3219); 'Invalid issue value in <medicine>' if balance>issued or issued<=0 (:3221-3222); 'You can only issue the prescribed qty' if issued != qty (:3224-3225). Negative-stock gate: if(pharmacyMedicine.getStock() < prescription.getQty()) throw 'Can not issue, insufficient stock in : <medicine>' (:3243-3244) — read-check-write on double, NO @Lock/pessimistic lock (race-prone). newStock = stock - qty (:3247), PharmacyStockCard OUT row written (:3252-3264). FEFO deductBatch: earliest-expiry batch, else lowest id (getEarlierBatch :3338-3376); records PrescriptionBatch per consumed batch (:3306-3329). Decrement hits the SINGLE pharmacy passed as pharmacy_id; issuePharmacy stamped (:3231). NO salesPharmacy split on the prescription path.

capabilities.name: OTC PharmacySaleOrder dispensing (giveMedicine) — states + non-functional batch FEFO
capabilities.description: Order must be APPROVED before issue 'Order not approved' (PatientResource.java:6230-6231). Per-detail state NOT-GIVEN → GIVEN (:6242,6259); guards mirror prescriptions: 'Could not issue medicine. Detail ... is not a pending' (:6242-6244), 'Invalid issue value in <medicine>' (:6249-6250), 'You can only issue the specified qty' (:6253-6254). Negative-stock gate 'Can not issue, insufficient stock in : <medicine>' (:6272-6273); aggregate PharmacyMedicine.stock decremented (:6276-6279), stock card OUT row written (:6281-6293). CRITICAL: OTC batch FEFO deductBatch is COMMENTED OUT (:6317 '//deductBatch...'), so OTC sales do NOT decrement PharmacyMedicineBatch rows — only aggregate stock moves. Field PharmacySaleOrderDetail.salesPharmacy EXISTS (PharmacySaleOrderDetail.java:76) but the decrement still uses the passed pharmacy_id, not salesPharmacy.

capabilities.name: Manual pharmacy stock update (update_stock)
capabilities.description: Primary non-transfer 'receiving' path: overwrites PharmacyMedicine.stock to the ABSOLUTE submitted value pm.getStock() (not additive), guards medicine active else 'Can not update stock. Medicine not active' (PharmacyResource.java:208-209) and 'Negative value is not allowed' if pm.getStock()<0 (:211-212); writes a PharmacyStockCard with qtyIn=balance=pm.getStock() and reference 'Stock Update' (:217-229). Gated by @PreAuthorize MEDICINE_STOCK-UPDATE (:200).

capabilities.name: Payroll state machine + net-salary math
capabilities.description: States: PENDING (on save, PayrollServiceImpl.java:59) → APPROVED (approve, :140) → SUBMITTED (submit, requires APPROVED, :191-198). Branches: VERIFIED (verify, requires PENDING, :103), CANCELED (cancel, requires PENDING, :169), RETURNED (from PENDING|VERIFIED, :219), REJECTED (from PENDING|VERIFIED, :240). LATENT BUG/AMBIGUITY: approve() requires status==PENDING (:127-128 'Could not approve. Only Pending payrolls can be approved') — the VERIFIED check is COMMENTED OUT (:124-126) — so a VERIFIED payroll can never be approved (dead-end). Math: gross = basicSalary + addOns (:275), net = gross - deductions (:276); approve re-asserts d.getNetSalary() != ((basicSalary+addOns)-deductions) → 'Invalid payroll details. Please check <empNo>' (:135-136, float != comparison). Negative net rejected 'Negative Net Salary is not allowed' (:278-279); negative inputs rejected 'Invalid Input' (:271-272). employerContributions captured but EXCLUDED from net. NO tax/PAYE/NSSF/pension engine — raw add-ons/deductions only. importEmployees pulls all active employees at basicSalary (:365-388). No disbursement/payment step beyond SUBMITTED.

capabilities.name: Inpatient admission → discharge state machine + bills-cleared gate
capabilities.description: Admission lifecycle: ADMITTED → STOPPED (set on discharge-plan creation, PatientResource.java:5269) → SIGNED-OUT (terminal, set in getDischargeSummary when bills cleared, :5361). Legacy terminal is SIGNED-OUT, NOT 'DISCHARGED'. Bills-cleared gate (applies to ALL patients, no CASH/INSURANCE branch): scans all admission PatientInvoice→PatientInvoiceDetail→PatientBill; if any bill status is UNPAID or VERIFIED → throw 'Could not get discharge summary. Patient have uncleared bills.' (:5346-5348). On success: all invoices flipped APPROVED (:5354-5356), admission SIGNED-OUT + dischargedBy/On/At stamped (:5361-5364), WardBed.status='EMPTY' (:5368-5370), Patient reset to type OUTPATIENT/paymentType CASH/insurancePlan null (:5372-5376), DischargePlan PENDING→APPROVED with approvedBy=createdBy (self-approval, :5378-5381). DischargePlan status PENDING set at save (:5284).

capabilities.name: Ward-bed charging math (flat, at admission)
capabilities.description: Ward bed billed as ONE flat PatientBill at bed assignment, qty=1, NOT per-night accrual. INSURANCE: picks the highest-priced eligible covered WardTypeInsurancePlan, or the patient's own plan if it matches and breaks (PatientServiceImpl.java:1801-1808); wardBedBill.amount=eligiblePlan.price, paid=price, balance=0, status COVERED (:1812-1818); added as PatientInvoiceDetail description 'Ward Bed / Room' qty 1 (:1847-1848). If the chosen eligible plan differs from the patient's plan AND wardType.price > eligible.price, a supplementary UNPAID 'Ward Bed / Room (Top up)' bill is raised for the difference (:1880-1889). CASH path raises UNPAID wardBedBill (:1760). AdmissionBed.status='OPENED' on assign (:1781). No multi-day loop anywhere.

capabilities.name: Nursing charts that DO exist in legacy
capabilities.description: Only three inpatient nursing-chart aggregates exist in domain: PatientVital (vitals; submit lifecycle PENDING→SUBMITTED→ARCHIVED with guards, PatientResource.java:1074,1181,1323-1339), PatientNursingCarePlan, PatientDressingChart. PatientPrescriptionChart exists for inpatient drug charting. These are the real nursing-chart surface to reproduce.

capabilities.name: Reporting surface (actual)
capabilities.description: Reports are predominantly inline repository queries inside ReportResource.java (~18 mapping endpoints) returning *Report/*CollectionModel DTOs (e.g. CollectionReport, GrnReport, LpoReport, ClinicianPerformanceReport, FastMovingDrugs). Only TWO dedicated report-service impls exist under reports/service: InventoryReportServiceImpl and LocalPurchaseOrderReportService. There is no 29-class report-service layer; 'report service' = inline query + DTO assembly. Depends on every upstream context's tables.

capabilities.name: Assets (master-data CRUD only)
capabilities.description: AssetResource exposes exactly 4 endpoints: GET /assets, GET /assets/get, POST /assets/save, POST /assets/delete (AssetResource.java:47,52,59,69). No status field, no lifecycle, no depreciation schedule, no asset-number generation, no asset-to-employee assignment workflow. save() is mis-gated with copy-paste @PreAuthorize EMPLOYEE-ALL (:60); delete() has its @PreAuthorize commented out (:70).

absentInLegacy: SPTO / PPTO document-number prefixes (Inc-08 §5, Legacy-finding-D): legacy uses 'SPT' for BOTH StoreToPharmacyTO (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:438) AND PharmacyToPharmacyTO (PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:428) — the collision is REAL legacy behaviour; SPTO/PPTO are NEW invented prefixes, not legacy.

Dedicated PostgreSQL sequences / DocumentNumberService with gap-free concurrency (Inc-08 §5, ADR-0009 §5): legacy uses Formater + getLastId()+1 (global MAX(id)+1) with NO zero-padding and NO per-day/fiscal reset (Formater.java:14-16; every requestRequest*No()).

Six-state Prescription lifecycle PENDING→ACCEPTED→HELD→VERIFIED→APPROVED→SOLD and PharmacySaleOrder mirror (Inc-08 scope): legacy prescription dispensing is only NOT-GIVEN→GIVEN (PatientResource.java:3217,3230); OTC detail only NOT-GIVEN→GIVEN under an APPROVED order (:6242,6259). No ACCEPTED/HELD/VERIFIED/SOLD/REJECTED dispensing states.

Per-line payStatus UNPAID→PAID gate on sell() (Inc-08 §1, PHARM-2): legacy issueMedicine/giveMedicine have NO pay-status check at dispense — payment is gated upstream at OPD order time; no per-line PAID flag in the dispensing path (PatientResource.java:3200-3293, 6212-6322).

FEFO with SELECT FOR UPDATE / pessimistic lock + atomic negative-stock under concurrency (Inc-08 §2, ADR-0017): legacy negative-stock guard is plain read-check-write on double with no locking (PatientResource.java:3243; StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:253); FEFO falls back to lowest-id when no expiry (getEarlierBatch).

'Stock cards update only on RN COMPLETED' (Inc-08 §3): FALSE for Store→Pharmacy — SOURCE store stock + store stock card are written at TO issue()/GOODS-ISSUED (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:253,262), only the DESTINATION pharmacy credit waits for RN approve (StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:206).
ThreeWayMatch / SupplierInvoice entity, three-way-match validate endpoint, payment-release gate (Inc-08 §8): Grep ThreeWayMatch|SupplierInvoice → ZERO files. Entirely absent in legacy.
LPO 'DRAFT' state (Inc-08 entity list DRAFT→PENDING→...): legacy LPO starts at PENDING; no DRAFT (LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl.java:84).

StockMovement append-only ledger with runningBalance + movement-type enum (RECEIPT/DISPENSE/TRANSFER_OUT/TRANSFER_IN/ADJUSTMENT/WASTAGE) (Inc-08 entities): legacy uses StoreStockCard / PharmacyStockCard rows with qtyIn/qtyOut/balance/reference free-text — no typed movement enum, no TRANSFER_IN/OUT/WASTAGE classification.

WardDayAccrualJob / nightly @Scheduled + ShedLock per-night ward accrual (Inc-07 JOB-001, ADR-0018; 3-night→3 InvoiceLines): Grep @Scheduled|ShedLock → ZERO files. Legacy charges ONE flat ward bill at admission (PatientServiceImpl.java:1812,1847). The planning doc itself concedes 'The legacy charges one flat amount at admission'.

Second-approver discharge gate (approvedBy != createdBy) / SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN (Inc-07 §3, M17): legacy getDischargeSummary self-approves, setting approvedBy=createdBy (PatientResource.java:5379). No distinct-approver rule exists.

Separate POST discharge-plan + POST discharge-plan/approve endpoints and a 'DISCHARGED' terminal state (Inc-07 scope): legacy uses a single GET /patients/get_discharge_summary that gates+flips, and the terminal admission state is 'SIGNED-OUT' (PatientResource.java:5329,5361), with intermediate 'STOPPED' (:5269).
