corrections: ReportResource endpoint count understated by ~half. The extraction states ReportResource has '~18 mapping endpoints' / '~18 endpoints' (capability 'Reporting surface (actual)', citation ReportResource.java:76-90). Direct count of @PostMapping report endpoints = 36 (ReportResource.java lines 164,191,217,281,290,299,314,387,400,466,505,540,563,581,599,630,661,674,720,766,812,858,903,948,997,1012,1028,1046,1068,1083,1092,1119,1151,1287,1452,1532; all @PostMapping, zero @GetMapping). The broader claims that reporting is inline-query-based and that there are only 2 dedicated report-service artifacts (not 29) remain correct, so this does not change the conclusion — but the '~18' figure is materially wrong and should read '~36 report endpoints'.

Imprecision: the extraction calls both InventoryReportServiceImpl AND LocalPurchaseOrderReportService 'dedicated report-service impls.' Verified: InventoryReportServiceImpl is a real impl class (InventoryReportServiceImpl.java:21 'public class InventoryReportServiceImpl implements InventoryReportService'), but LocalPurchaseOrderReportService.java:17 is an INTERFACE only ('public interface LocalPurchaseOrderReportService') with NO impl class found anywhere (Grep 'implements LocalPurchaseOrderReportService|LocalPurchaseOrderReportServiceImpl' -> no files). So reports/service contains exactly: InventoryReportService (interface), InventoryReportServiceImpl (impl), LocalPurchaseOrderReportService (interface, no impl), package-info. The '2 dedicated report-service classes, not 29' headline holds; the word 'impls' for the second item is inaccurate.

Scope clarification (not a defect, but downstream-readers should note): the absence claim 'No ACCEPTED/HELD/...SOLD dispensing states' is correct ONLY as scoped to the prescription/sale-order dispensing path (issueMedicine PatientResource.java:3217,3230; giveMedicine :6242,6259 are strictly NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN). However 'ACCEPTED' and 'HELD' DO exist elsewhere in PatientResource.java for OTHER lifecycles: ACCEPTED on lab-test orders (PatientResource.java:3909,3922,3947,4013,4047) and radiology orders (:4221,4234,4259,4280,4305,4325); HELD on a status-filter list (:329) and on admission/consultation hold (:5725,5753,5891,5914). 'SOLD' is genuinely absent (zero hits). The extraction's dispensing-scoped claim is true; just ensure readers do not over-read it as 'ACCEPTED/HELD absent from the whole system.'
unverifiableClaims: PatientVital full lifecycle 'PENDING->SUBMITTED->ARCHIVED' — I directly verified the SUBMITTED-guard -> ARCHIVED transition (PatientResource.java:1323-1339) and the PatientVital entity exists, but I did not open the cited :1074 and :1181 to confirm the PENDING and SUBMITTED set-points. The entity and the ARCHIVED transition are confirmed; the earlier two states are stated but not re-verified by me.

Service-class line-count figures (e.g., PharmacyResource '346 lines', InternalOrderResource '~1400 lines', LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl '336 ln', PayrollServiceImpl '412 ln', AssetResource '86 ln') were not independently measured; I verified the cited line NUMBERS resolve to the claimed code (e.g., AssetResource ends at line 85, consistent with '86 ln'; InternalOrderResource has 50 mapping endpoints as claimed), but exact total-line counts were not separately counted.

The Angular-side absence of device-fingerprint/device-binding: I confirmed absence in the backend com.orbix.api tree (Grep returned no files for fingerprint|deviceId|deviceBinding|getRemoteAddr|X-Device|userAgent). The extraction's scope statement also references the Angular app, which is outside the cited legacy-root path and was not scanned in this verification; the backend-side absence is fully confirmed.
verification.summary: Verdict: ACCURATE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. I opened every cited file and checked the load-bearing claims line-by-line; the extraction is highly reliable and its core conclusions stand. All four phantom-feature absence claims are confirmed by direct Grep returning zero files: no @Audited anywhere in com.orbix.api (Envers inert), no device-fingerprint/device-binding (fingerprint|deviceId|deviceBinding|getRemoteAddr|X-Device|userAgent), no ThreeWayMatch|SupplierInvoice, no @Scheduled|ShedLock. Other confirmed absences: LPO 'DRAFT' (zero hits), StockMovement entity (no file), MedicationAdministration/FluidBalance/CareActivity entities (no files), 'SOLD' dispensing state (zero hits). State machines verified line-exact: LPO PENDING->VERIFIED->APPROVED->SUBMITTED with RETURNED/REJECTED branches (LocalPurchaseOrderServiceImpl.java:84,124,152,180,201,222); GRN PENDING->APPROVED with one-GRN-per-LPO guard and atomic stock credit + LPO->RECEIVED (GoodsReceivedNoteServiceImpl.java:68,77,117,121,128,184,194; class-level @Transactional line 47); Store->Pharmacy TO/RN with SOURCE stock decremented at TO issue/GOODS-ISSUED not at RN (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:86,186,208,230,253,256; RN credit StoreToPharmacyRNServiceImpl.java:206-228; RN guard + COMPLETED InternalOrderResource.java:725,731,739,745); Payroll incl. the real latent dead-end bug where the VERIFIED check is commented out so approve() requires PENDING (PayrollServiceImpl.java:124-128); Inpatient STOPPED->SIGNED-OUT with the all-patients bills-cleared gate and self-approval discharge (PatientResource.java:5269,5346-5348,5354-5381); flat one-bill ward charging with insurance top-up (PatientServiceImpl.java:1757-1889). Numbering verified exactly incl. the genuine REAL prefix collision: both StoreToPharmacyTO and PharmacyToPharmacyTO emit 'SPT' (StoreToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:438, PharmacyToPharmacyTOServiceImpl.java:428); Formater.formatWithCurrentDate = prefix+yyyyMMdd(now)+'-'+suffix with NO zero-padding/reset (Formater.java:14-16, at com.orbix.api.accessories); getLastId()=SELECT MAX(id) confirming the race-prone global counter. Dispensing is strictly NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN and OTC deductBatch is commented out (PatientResource.java:3217,3230,3243,3288,6230,6242,6259,6317). update_stock does an absolute overwrite (PharmacyResource.java:214); AssetResource is bare 4-endpoint CRUD mis-gated EMPLOYEE-ALL with delete @PreAuthorize commented out (AssetResource.java:47,52,59,60,69,70). TWO corrections: (1) ReportResource has 36 report endpoints, not the claimed '~18'; (2) LocalPurchaseOrderReportService is an interface with no impl, so calling it a second 'impl' is imprecise (only InventoryReportServiceImpl is a real impl). Both are minor and do not undermine the extraction's conclusions. Confidence in the extraction: justifiably HIGH.

alreadyBuilt: Lab order lifecycle PENDING->ACCEPTED->COLLECTED->VERIFIED with REJECTED branch and HOLD revert-to-PENDING — SHIPPED inc-05 C7 as the non-polymorphic LabTest entity (LabTestController accept/reject/collect/verify/hold endpoints; LabTest.java domain). Legacy-accurate states; no COMPLETED.

Radiology order lifecycle PENDING->ACCEPTED->VERIFIED (direct, no live collect) with REJECTED branch and HOLD — SHIPPED inc-05 C8 (RadiologyController; Radiology.java; COLLECTED retained dead-state for legacy data fidelity). Matches verified legacy radiology = ACCEPTED->VERIFIED direct (verify_radiology guards ACCEPTED, PatientResource.java:4280).

Procedure order lifecycle PENDING->ACCEPTED->VERIFIED (no APPROVED, no reject/hold/collect endpoints) — SHIPPED inc-05 C9 (ProcedureController; Procedure.java; ProcedureStatus PENDING/ACCEPTED/REJECTED/VERIFIED). add_note settlement gate reproduced (ACCEPTED+settled->VERIFIED, 422 'Payment not verified').

Lab save-result (COLLECTED-only, no status change) and verify writing free-text result/level/testRange(rrange)/unit — SHIPPED inc-05 (PUT /lab-tests/uid/{uid}/result; verify). Flat columns, no per-analyte lines, no server-side flag computation (matches legacy).
Lab separate report field + report endpoint — SHIPPED inc-05 (PUT /lab-tests/uid/{uid}/report, separate from result).

Lab/Radiology named attachments: max 5, per-type child tables (lab_test_attachments / radiology_attachments), add-gate (Lab=COLLECTED, Radiology=ACCEPTED), delete-blocked-when-VERIFIED, download-gated-VERIFIED — SHIPPED inc-05 (LabTestController/RadiologyController attachment endpoints; V24/V25). Matches legacy per-type tables + VERIFIED delete-lock (PatientResource.java:6021/6154).
Radiology inline attachment BYTEA blob set at verify — SHIPPED inc-05 (Radiology.attachment byte[]), separate from named attachments; mirrors legacy Radiology Byte[] column.

Per-insurance-plan vs cash pricing at order-raise (INSURANCE/COVERED->settled, CASH-OPD->unsettled) via billing.api recordClinicalCharge — SHIPPED inc-04/inc-05 (BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge; SettlementPolicy; ServicePrice matrix from inc-02). Covered-override / cash-fallback logic lives in billing consumer code.

Settlement enforcement (the legacy worklist payment FILTER) — SHIPPED inc-05 as local settled flag: worklists filter settled=true (Lab/Radiology/Procedure); consultation open_consultation is the only hard gate (CR-INC05-01). Legacy parity: payment is a worklist visibility filter, NOT an accept/verify precondition.
Duplicate-order guard (same type on same encounter -> 422) — SHIPPED inc-05 for Lab/Radiology/Procedure (matches legacy 'Duplicate ... Types is not allowed').
Rejection-with-comment, cleared on accept (Lab); Radiology accept does NOT clear rejectComment (asymmetry preserved) — SHIPPED inc-05 (legacy-verified asymmetry).

Three SEPARATE order tables (lab_tests/radiologies/procedures), NOT a polymorphic ClinicalOrder; results as columns, NOT a separate result entity — SHIPPED inc-05 (the exact opposite of the inc-06 doc's ClinicalOrder/LabResultLine claim, and matches legacy three-entity reality).

Theatre as master-data only (code/name/description/location/active; ADMIN-ACCESS mutations; no price, no scheduling, no relationships) — SHIPPED inc-02/inc-05 (TheatreController; Theatre.java; V6). Procedure.theatreUid is a nullable loose ref (matches legacy Theatre-as-tag).

Encounter binding exactly-one (consultation FK | non-consultation FK | admission_uid loose) with DB CHECK num_nonnulls=1 — SHIPPED inc-05 for all three order types (matches legacy exactly-one-of property rule).
Delete PENDING-only for Lab/Radiology/Procedure — SHIPPED inc-05 (matches legacy 'only a PENDING ... can be deleted').

Authenticated-only access, NO @PreAuthorize privilege codes on these clinical lifecycle endpoints — SHIPPED inc-05 per CR-INC05-02 (matches legacy: lab/rad/proc lifecycle endpoints carry NO @PreAuthorize).

genuineGaps.gap: Lab/Radiology/Procedure DELETE must raise a PatientCreditNote (status PENDING, PCN number, reference 'Canceled lab test'/'Canceled radiology'/'Canceled procedure') when a PatientPaymentDetail with status RECEIVED exists, and must reverse the PatientInvoiceDetail / delete the empty PatientInvoice / delete the PatientPaymentDetail before deleting the order+bill. As-built inc-05 explicitly DEFERRED this credit-note seam (delete PENDING-only, 'credit-note for paid bills deferred'). The PCN instrument itself exists in inc-04, but the clinical delete-time reversal wiring is not built.
genuineGaps.belongsToContext: Laboratory / Radiology / Procedures (delete path) coordinating with Billing (PatientCreditNote)

genuineGaps.gap: Radiology stand-alone 'add report' endpoint gated on BILL status (PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED) and independent of order status — legacy radiologies/add_report writes Radiology.report at any order status as long as the bill is paid/covered/verified, throwing 'Could not add report. Payment not verified' otherwise. As-built inc-05 Radiology only writes report inline at verify (ACCEPTED->VERIFIED); there is no separate bill-gated add_report endpoint as Lab has.

Post-VERIFIED report amendment via the bill-gated add_report path — legacy add_report (both lab and radiology) is gated ONLY on bill status, so the report narrative can be overwritten even after the order is VERIFIED (result/range/level/unit cannot, but report can). As-built inc-05 gates Lab report writes to COLLECTED-only and Radiology has no separate report endpoint, so this verified-order report amendment behaviour is not reproduced. (Flag to legacy-analyst/healthcare-domain-expert as a known legacy asymmetry to confirm before building.)
genuineGaps.legacyCitation: PatientResource.java:3381-3395 (lab add_report bill-gated), 3183-3197 (radiology add_report bill-gated); add_report never checks order status

genuineGaps.gap: save_reason_for_rejection as a discrete endpoint that sets rejectComment ONLY when current status==REJECTED (else 'Could not save. Only allowed for rejected tests'). As-built inc-05 sets rejectComment inline at reject time; the separate, status-guarded reason-edit endpoint (lab_tests/save_reason_for_rejection and radiologies/save_reason_for_rejection) is not reproduced as an independent operation.

Attachment file STORAGE + download streaming. Legacy persists bytes to local filesystem at CompanyProfile.publicPath (app-generated filename 'LT'/'RAD'+id+patientNo+random+timestamp, 10 MiB cap) and streams inline on download. As-built inc-05 attachment tables store only name + fileName REFERENCES ('actual file storage OUT OF SCOPE per domain comments') — no byte persistence, no upload-bytes handling, no download endpoint, no 10 MiB size cap. A real storage mechanism (legacy local-disk equivalent or an approved modern StoragePort — note StoragePort/MinIO/S3 is NEW SCOPE, see drift) plus the download endpoint must still be built.
genuineGaps.legacyCitation: PatientServiceImpl.java:2823-2906 (lab upload/storage), 2922-2996 (radiology); PatientResource.java:5960-6007 (lab download), 6093-6140 (radiology download); 10485760-byte cap

genuineGaps.gap: Inpatient / admission-scoped order paths for Lab/Radiology/Procedure. Legacy supports ordering against an admission (admission must be IN-PROCESS; PENDING admission -> 'Admission not verified'; inpatient billing path sets bill status VERIFIED when no covered plan), and has an inpatient worklist (get_lab_inpatient_list, bill in {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED}). As-built inc-05 has admission_uid as a loose deferred column with NO admission-scoped endpoints, NO inpatient worklist, NO admission verification gate, and the by-patient query CR-INC05-15 still returns admission orders instead of excluding them. Explicitly deferred to the Inpatient/Nursing increment.
genuineGaps.legacyCitation: PatientServiceImpl.java:804-810 (admission gate, lab), 1031-1058 (radiology), 1292-1307 (procedure); PatientResource.java:3685-3700 (lab inpatient worklist, {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED}), 3761 (radiology inpatient)

genuineGaps.gap: Cascade cleanup of orders on consultation/non-consultation cancel and sign-off: on cancel, PENDING orders with UNPAID/null-status bills are hard-deleted with their bills; on sign-off, UNPAID order bills are flipped to 'CANCELED'; and sign-off/referral is BLOCKED when any order bill is UNPAID ('Could not save. Patient have uncleared lab/radiology/procedure bill(s)'). The inc-05 closure paths handle deceased/referral with bill-gate asymmetry (CR-INC05-09), but the per-order cascade-delete-on-cancel and UNPAID->CANCELED-on-signoff behaviour for lab/rad/proc bills should be confirmed as wired; treat as a gap pending verification.
genuineGaps.belongsToContext: Laboratory / Radiology / Procedures (encounter-cascade) intersecting Clinical/OPD and Billing

genuineGaps.gap: Department-side reports that read lab/rad/proc data: get_lab_tests_by_date / lab_test_statistics_report (VERIFIED rows), lab_sample_collection_report (all statuses), and the collection reports (lab/radiology/procedure_collection_report) with half-open date window from.atStartOfDay()..to.plusDays(1) and per-cashier Collection mapping. None of these report endpoints are built (Reporting is inc-10). Listed here so they are not mistaken for inc-06 scope, but they ARE genuine legacy capabilities awaiting the Reporting increment.

planningDocDrift: Polymorphic ClinicalOrder aggregate with a 'kind' discriminator (LAB_TEST/RADIOLOGY/PROCEDURE) — PHANTOM. Legacy keeps three fully separate entities/tables (LabTest, Radiology, Procedure), each with its own status string and endpoints (PatientResource.java). inc-05 already shipped this correctly as three separate order tables; the doc's ClinicalOrder must NOT be built.

Lifecycle 'Lab/Radiology: PENDING -> ACCEPTED -> COMPLETED (/CANCELLED)' — CONTRADICTS legacy. There is NO COMPLETED status and NO CANCELLED status on any order. Legacy lab terminal is VERIFIED via PENDING->ACCEPTED->COLLECTED->VERIFIED (with REJECTED branch + HOLD revert); the doc collapses COLLECTED+VERIFIED into a non-existent COMPLETED and omits COLLECTED and HOLD entirely. Radiology terminal is VERIFIED reached ACCEPTED->VERIFIED directly. Cancellation is a hard delete, not a status. (PatientResource.java:3960-3983, 4272-4294)

Procedure 'PENDING -> APPROVED (surgeon/anaesthetist; M14) -> COMPLETED' with a distinct approve() endpoint requiring PROCEDURE_APPROVE + SURGEON/ANAESTHETIST role and hmis.procedure.allow-self-approve config — PHANTOM. Legacy procedure has NO APPROVED state and NO approve endpoint; the only path to its terminal (VERIFIED) is procedures/add_note. The 'M14 missing APPROVED' claim is an invention; all setStatus('APPROVED') in legacy operate on Invoice/DischargePlan/DeceasedNote objects, never Procedure. inc-05 correctly shipped Procedure with no APPROVED state. (PatientResource.java:4026-4067, 3397-3416)

OperativeRecord aggregate (findings/technique/instruments/complications/surgeon signature) required before complete(), HTTP 422 OPERATIVE_RECORD_MISSING — PHANTOM. Zero operative/surgeon/surgery files in legacy; the only procedure narrative is the free-text Procedure.note (max 10000) set via add_note. No surgeon role/assignment/signature exists.

Per-analyte LabResultLine linked to MdLabTestAnalyte, with server-side ReferenceRange flag computation (NORMAL/LOW/HIGH/CRITICAL_LOW/CRITICAL_HIGH/ABNORMAL) resolved by (analyteUid, sex, ageAtOrderDate) — PHANTOM. Legacy stores ONE flat result with free-text range/level/unit taken verbatim from the client; there is NO LabResultLine entity, NO MdReferenceRange evaluation, NO flag enum. LabTestTypeRange is a bare named-string label never used to evaluate results. The 'golden-master flag test' targets behaviour that does not exist. (PatientResource.java:3971-3974, 3996-3999; LabTestTypeRange.java:37-54)

LabBatch grouping by (labTestTypeUid, orderDate) with atomic bulk accept/complete and seq_lab_batch_no producing 'LABB{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}' — PHANTOM. No LabBatch entity, no batch endpoints, no bulk transition, and NO lab document/batch number anywhere; a lab order has only a DB identity id (and a ULID uid in the rebuild). Every lab test is accepted/collected/verified individually.

Generic ADR-0015 'attachment' table keyed (ownerKind=CLINICAL_ORDER, ownerUid) with StoragePort -> MinIO/S3, ClamAV scan, opaque storage_key, and HTTP 302 pre-signed download redirect — NOT legacy behaviour (NEW SCOPE / modernization). Legacy uses dedicated per-type tables (lab_test_attachments, radiology_attachments) with local-disk storage and inline streaming. inc-05 already shipped the per-type tables (the exact narrow scope the doc says to avoid). MinIO/S3/ClamAV/pre-signed are net-new design choices that must go through change-request control, not be presented as legacy reproduction. The doc's instruction 'do not create a lab_test_attachment table' directly contradicts both legacy and the already-shipped inc-05 design.

Attachment delete-lock 'no delete after COMPLETED or CANCELLED' with type=urn:hmis:error:ATTACHMENT_DELETE_LOCKED, citing PatientResource.java:6021-6023 — WRONG status value and WRONG cite semantics. The legacy guard blocks delete when the owning order status == VERIFIED ('Could not delete. Lab Test already verified' at 6021-6022; radiology 'Radiology already verified' at 6154-6156). There is no COMPLETED/CANCELLED status. inc-05 already shipped the correct VERIFIED delete-lock.

CASH payment gate as a HARD precondition on accept()/complete() returning HTTP 409 urn:hmis:error:ORDER_NOT_SETTLED, with a 'settled' boolean written by SettlementDispatcher gating transitions — CONTRADICTS legacy. Legacy enforces payment ONLY as a worklist inclusion FILTER (bill.status in PAID/COVERED[/VERIFIED]) and at report entry; accept/collect/verify perform NO bill/payment check. The 'M3/M13/DIAG-2: settlement check as a worklist filter only is a pitfall to avoid' framing is itself the inversion of legacy truth — the worklist filter IS the legacy behaviour. inc-05 correctly reproduced this via the local settled flag + worklist filter (CR-INC05-01), with the only hard gate at consultation open. The doc's ORDER_NOT_SETTLED transition gate must NOT be built without an approved change request.

Mandatory ACCEPTED step for radiology before complete (M16/DIAG-1: 'PENDING->COMPLETED direct transition forbidden ... Radiology must assert status==ACCEPTED') — PARTIALLY WRONG for radiology. Legacy radiology does NOT require COLLECTED and the doc conflates this; more importantly there is no COMPLETED transition at all. The ACCEPTED-before-result gate is real for radiology (verify requires ACCEPTED) and lab (verify requires COLLECTED which requires ACCEPTED), but the doc's framing around a COMPLETED state is phantom. (verify_radiology guards ACCEPTED at PatientResource.java:4280; verify_lab_test guards COLLECTED at 3968)

Theatre scheduling subsystem: schedule endpoint, theatreUid/scheduledAt/scheduledByUsername on orders, double-booking note — PHANTOM. Theatre is pure master-data; Procedure references a theatre only as an optional tag by name/uid with NO scheduling timestamps, slots, availability, or booking logic. inc-05 shipped Theatre as master-data only and Procedure.theatreUid as a nullable loose ref (correct).

177 @PreAuthorize privilege codes on every endpoint (LAB_TEST-CREATE/LAB_TEST-VIEW/PROCEDURE_APPROVE etc.) — PHANTOM count and PHANTOM enforcement. Legacy lab/rad/proc lifecycle endpoints have NO @PreAuthorize (commented out / absent); IAM has 35 privilege codes (26 active), not 177. inc-05 correctly shipped authenticated-only with no privilege gates per CR-INC05-02. RBAC privilege codes here would be NEW SCOPE requiring change-request approval, and the '177' figure is fabricated.

Unified ServicePrice(planUid,kind,serviceUid,currency) + PriceLookup.resolve() with cash-fallback presented as inc-06 scope — already SHIPPED in inc-02 (as a row-lookup matrix) with the resolve-TIME billing math (cash-then-covered override, inpatient cash-VERIFIED fallback) shipped in inc-04/inc-05 consumer code. Not an inc-06 gap. (Legacy had two separate sources: type.price + *TypeInsurancePlan.price keyed on covered=true; the unified matrix is an approved modernization, not legacy.)

Spring Modulith @ApplicationModuleListener after-commit audit events (LabOrderAccepted, LabOrderCompleted, RadiologyOrderAccepted/Completed, ProcedureOrderApproved/Completed) — partly PHANTOM/NEW SCOPE. Legacy emits NO domain events and has NO Envers (@Audited = zero across com.orbix.api); audit is only in-row actor/day/timestamp stamp columns. The stamp-column audit IS shipped in inc-05; a tamper-evident event/audit baseline is net-new (ADR-0007) and must not be framed as legacy reproduction. Events named after non-existent states (Completed/Approved) are doubly invalid.

ClinicalOrder.settled written by SettlementDispatcher in 'billing -> laboratory/radiology/procedures' direction with three sibling modules — DRIFT vs as-built. inc-05 shipped the settled flag on each clinical entity, written by ConsultationSettlementListener consuming BillSettledEvent (billing -> clinical), inside a single clinical module (not three sibling laboratory/radiology/procedures modules). The doc's module decomposition into three separate top-level modules contradicts the shipped single clinical module governed by ADR-0022.
reconciliation.recommendation: Do NOT run inc-06 as a build increment against this planning doc — it is heavily drifted and ~80% of its 'new' scope is either already shipped in inc-05 (under legacy-accurate names: three separate order tables, VERIFIED terminal, COLLECTED step, HOLD revert, per-type attachments, settled-flag worklist filter, no-APPROVED procedure, Theatre-as-masterdata) or is phantom/contradictory (ClinicalOrder, LabResultLine + server-side flags, LabBatch + seq_lab_batch_no, OperativeRecord, procedure APPROVED/surgeon-approval, theatre scheduling, COMPLETED/CANCELLED states, ORDER_NOT_SETTLED hard gate, 177 privilege codes, MinIO/S3/ClamAV/pre-signed attachments). Recommended path: (1) Mark the inc-06 planning doc 'SUPERSEDED IN PART' (same treatment given to the inc-05 planning doc) and rewrite it from the verified legacy extractions and the inc-05 ratified decisions before any code is written — consistent with the locked 'discovery-before-code' pattern used for inc-01..05. (2) Re-scope the genuine remaining work into the increments where it actually belongs: the credit-note-on-delete reversal, radiology bill-gated add_report, save_reason_for_rejection endpoints, post-VERIFIED report amendment, and real attachment file storage+download are small/medium clinical-order completion items that close inc-05 deferrals (raise CRs to pull them forward or fold into a clinical-order-fulfilment top-up); admission-scoped order paths + inpatient worklists are LARGE and belong to the Inpatient/Nursing increment (07); the lab/rad/proc report endpoints belong to Reporting (10). (3) For any genuinely modern capability the client still wants (generic attachment + object storage + virus scan, tamper-evident audit events, RBAC privilege codes on clinical endpoints, batch grouping), open explicit NEW SCOPE entries in the change-request register with engagement-lead approval — these must NOT carry acceptance criteria written as legacy reproduction. (4) Author acceptance criteria only from the verified extractions (exact states, exact error strings incl. the legacy copy-paste 'Could not accept' verbs, exact bill-status gates), never from this planning doc's invented states/errors.

recommendedScope: INC-06A (clinical-order fulfilment top-up — close inc-05 deferrals; SMALL/MEDIUM; this is the WHOLE of the real "inc-06" remainder). Mark docs/delivery/increments/06-lab-radiology-procedure-theatre.md as 'SUPERSEDED IN PART' (same treatment as the inc-05 planning doc) and re-author its acceptance criteria from the verified legacy extractions + the inc-05 ratified decisions before any code is written. ITEM 1 (MEDIUM, now UNBLOCKED — top priority): Wire the Lab/Radiology/Procedure DELETE credit-note + invoice reversal seam. The blocking dependency the inc-05 TODOs cited ('billing.api does not yet publish a cancel/credit-note command') NO LONGER HOLDS — BillingCommands.cancelCharge(billUid, reference, ctx) is now published (backend/src/main/java/com/otapp/hmis/billing/api/BillingCommands.java:58) and CreditNoteService.cancelCharge already performs the exact legacy delete-time reversal (soft-cancel bill→CANCELED, refund RECEIVED→REFUNDED, raise PENDING PatientCreditNote, detach invoice-detail, delete empty invoice). Replace the three TODOs (LabTestService.java:449-450; ProcedureService.java:349; equivalent in RadiologyService) with a cancelCharge call using the legacy reference labels 'Canceled lab test' / 'Canceled radiology' / 'Canceled procedure'. Keep the legacy soft-flag pattern (CreditNoteService already standardised this — do NOT reproduce the legacy hard-delete of bill/payment-detail).

ITEM 2 (SMALL): Radiology stand-alone bill-gated add_report endpoint. As-built RadiologyService writes report inline only at verify (ACCEPTED→VERIFIED). Add a separate report endpoint gated on BILL status (PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED) and INDEPENDENT of order status, mirroring legacy radiologies/add_report (PatientResource.java:3183-3197). Note this requires a read of bill status — confirm the seam shape with billing (a bill-status query on billing.api) since the clinical module currently only holds the local 'settled' flag and never reads billing bill status post-hoc (ADR-0008 §6). Flag the cross-module read as an architecture decision before building.

ITEM 3 (SMALL): save_reason_for_rejection as a discrete, status-guarded endpoint for Lab AND Radiology (sets rejectComment ONLY when current status==REJECTED, else verbatim 'Could not save. Only allowed for rejected tests'). Legacy: PatientResource.java:2034-2048 (lab), 2018-2032 (radiology). As-built sets rejectComment inline at reject time only.

ITEM 4 (SMALL, GATED — needs HDE/legacy-analyst confirmation first): Post-VERIFIED report amendment via the bill-gated add_report path (report narrative can be overwritten after VERIFIED; result/range/level/unit cannot). This is a confirmed-but-asymmetric legacy behaviour (PatientResource.java:3381-3395 lab, 3183-3197 radiology). Do NOT build until legacy-analyst + healthcare-domain-expert confirm this asymmetry is intended-to-reproduce vs a legacy defect; route the confirmation request through engagement-lead.

ITEM 5 (MEDIUM, partially NEW SCOPE — split it): Attachment file STORAGE + download streaming. As-built attachment tables store name + fileName references only ('file storage OUT OF SCOPE'); there is no byte persistence, no upload handling, no download endpoint, no 10 MiB cap. Build the download endpoint + size cap + actual byte persistence. The STORAGE BACKEND choice is a fork: (a) legacy parity = local-disk at a configured public path with inline streaming and the app-generated filename scheme; (b) the ratified target (ADR-0015) = S3-compatible object storage + virus-scan. ADR-0015 is approved in overview.md but its first concrete implementation is explicitly 'pinned during the relevant context build' and 'no attachment table is authored until ADR-0015 is approved'. Decision needed from engagement owner on whether inc-06A pins ADR-0015's first use here (object storage) or ships legacy-parity local-disk now and defers object storage. Either way the DOWNLOAD + size-cap behaviour is legacy reproduction; the object-store/virus-scan is the net-new ADR-0015 capability.

ITEM 6 (MEDIUM, verification-first): Confirm whether encounter cancel/sign-off cascade for lab/rad/proc bills is wired (on cancel: PENDING orders with UNPAID/null bills hard-deleted with bills; on sign-off: UNPAID order bills flipped to CANCELED; sign-off/referral BLOCKED on any UNPAID order bill — 'Could not save. Patient have uncleared lab/radiology/procedure bill(s)'). inc-05 closure handled deceased/referral with bill-gate asymmetry (CR-INC05-09) but the per-order cascade for L/R/P bills should be verified as wired; treat as a gap only if verification shows it absent. Legacy: PatientResource.java:434-494, 701-720, 5465-5489.

AFTER inc-06A: proceed to Pharmacy, Inventory & Procurement (inc-08) as the next FULL bounded-context build — it is the true topological next un-built context (see topological note). Run discovery-before-code on 08 first (its planning doc and the inc-07 doc both carry the same kind of drift catalogued in the legacy survey).
rejectedFromPlanningDoc: Polymorphic ClinicalOrder aggregate with a kind discriminator (LAB_TEST/RADIOLOGY/PROCEDURE) — PHANTOM. Legacy and as-built both keep three separate entities/tables (LabTest, Radiology, Procedure). Already shipped correctly in inc-05.

Lifecycle 'PENDING → ACCEPTED → COMPLETED (/CANCELLED)' — CONTRADICTS legacy. There is NO COMPLETED and NO CANCELLED status on any order. Lab terminal is VERIFIED via PENDING→ACCEPTED→COLLECTED→VERIFIED (+REJECTED branch + HOLD revert); radiology terminal is VERIFIED via ACCEPTED→VERIFIED direct. Cancellation is a hard delete, not a status.

Procedure APPROVED state + distinct approve() endpoint requiring PROCEDURE_APPROVE + SURGEON/ANAESTHETIST role + hmis.procedure.allow-self-approve config — PHANTOM. Legacy procedure has no APPROVED state and no approve endpoint; the only path to VERIFIED is procedures/add_note. The 'M14 missing APPROVED' claim is an invention.

OperativeRecord aggregate (findings/technique/instruments/complications/surgeon signature) required before complete(), HTTP 422 OPERATIVE_RECORD_MISSING — PHANTOM. Zero operative/surgeon/surgery files in legacy; the only procedure narrative is free-text Procedure.note via add_note.

Per-analyte LabResultLine linked to MdLabTestAnalyte + server-side ReferenceRange flag computation (NORMAL/LOW/HIGH/CRITICAL_LOW/CRITICAL_HIGH/ABNORMAL) by (analyteUid,sex,age) — PHANTOM. Legacy stores ONE flat free-text result/range/level/unit verbatim from the client; LabTestTypeRange is a bare named-string label never used to evaluate results. The 'golden-master flag test' targets behaviour that does not exist.

LabBatch grouping by (labTestTypeUid, orderDate) + atomic bulk accept/complete + seq_lab_batch_no producing 'LABB{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}' — PHANTOM. No batch entity, no bulk transition, no lab document number anywhere; identity is the DB id (+ULID uid).

Generic ADR-0015 'attachment' table keyed (ownerKind=CLINICAL_ORDER, ownerUid) presented as legacy reproduction — WRONG FRAMING. Legacy uses dedicated per-type tables (lab_test_attachments, radiology_attachments), already shipped in inc-05. MinIO/S3/ClamAV/pre-signed are net-new ADR-0015 capabilities that must go through change-request control as NEW SCOPE, not be presented as legacy parity. The doc's instruction 'do not create a lab_test_attachment table' contradicts both legacy and the shipped inc-05 design.

Attachment delete-lock keyed on COMPLETED/CANCELLED status with type urn:hmis:error:ATTACHMENT_DELETE_LOCKED — WRONG status value and WRONG cite semantics. Legacy blocks delete when owning order status == VERIFIED ('Could not delete. Lab Test already verified' PatientResource.java:6021-6022; radiology 6154-6156). Already shipped correctly in inc-05.

CASH payment gate as a HARD precondition on accept()/complete() returning HTTP 409 urn:hmis:error:ORDER_NOT_SETTLED, with a SettlementDispatcher-written 'settled' boolean gating transitions — CONTRADICTS legacy. Legacy enforces payment ONLY as a worklist inclusion FILTER (bill.status in PAID/COVERED[/VERIFIED]) and at report entry; accept/collect/verify perform NO bill check. inc-05 reproduced this correctly via the local settled flag + worklist filter (CR-INC05-01). The 'settlement-filter is a pitfall' framing is the inversion of legacy truth.

Theatre scheduling subsystem (schedule endpoint, theatreUid/scheduledAt/scheduledByUsername on orders, double-booking detection) — PHANTOM. Theatre is pure master-data; Procedure references a theatre only as an optional tag with no scheduling/slots/availability. Already shipped correctly in inc-05.

177 (or per-endpoint LAB_TEST-CREATE/LAB_TEST-VIEW/PROCEDURE_APPROVE) @PreAuthorize privilege codes on every clinical lifecycle endpoint — PHANTOM enforcement. Legacy lab/rad/proc lifecycle endpoints carry NO @PreAuthorize. inc-05 shipped authenticated-only per CR-INC05-02. RBAC privilege gates here would be NEW SCOPE requiring change-request approval; the '177-codes-gate-every-endpoint' figure is misapplied (it is the system-wide ADR-0006 count, not a per-endpoint requirement on these endpoints).

Spring Modulith @ApplicationModuleListener after-commit audit events named LabOrderAccepted/LabOrderCompleted/RadiologyOrderAccepted/Completed/ProcedureOrderApproved/Completed — partly PHANTOM / NEW SCOPE. Legacy emits no domain events and has no Envers (@Audited = zero). The in-row stamp-column audit + the net-new append-only audit_log (ADR-0007) are already the chosen mechanism; events named after non-existent states (Completed/Approved) are doubly invalid.

Unified ServicePrice(planUid,kind,serviceUid,currency) + PriceLookup.resolve() with cash-fallback presented as inc-06 scope — ALREADY SHIPPED in inc-02 (row-lookup matrix) with resolve-time billing math in inc-04/inc-05 consumer code. Not an inc-06 gap.

Three sibling top-level modules (laboratory/radiology/procedures) with a SettlementDispatcher writing ClinicalOrder.settled in the billing→{lab,rad,proc} direction — DRIFT vs as-built. inc-05 shipped the settled flag on each clinical entity inside a SINGLE clinical module, written by ConsultationSettlementListener consuming BillSettledEvent (ADR-0022). The module map (overview.md) lists laboratory/radiology/procedures as separate contexts on paper, but the shipped clinical module governs them; do not re-split without an ADR change.
scope.topologicalNote: The clinical L/R/P/Theatre order+result+attachment+worklist loops are SHIPPED (inc-05). What the inc-06 doc calls 'new' is ~80% already-built-under-legacy-accurate-names or phantom; the genuine remainder is a thin set of inc-05 closure items (recommendedScope ITEMs 1-6) totalling SMALL/MEDIUM. Build order for the remainder: ITEM 1 (credit-note-on-delete) is unblocked NOW because billing.api.cancelCharge landed after the inc-05 TODOs were written — it depends only on billing (already built, inc-04) and clinical (inc-05); no new context needed. ITEMs 2/4 depend on a billing bill-status read seam that does not yet exist (clinical currently holds only the local settled flag and by ADR-0008 §6 never reads billing bill status post-hoc) — that seam is a small architecture decision to ratify before building. ITEM 5's object-storage half depends on pinning ADR-0015's first concrete implementation. The LARGE deferred item (admission-scoped L/R/P order paths + inpatient worklists + admission-verification gate) is NOT inc-06 — it belongs to Inpatient/Nursing (inc-07), which itself depends on Pharmacy/Inventory (inc-08) for ConsumableStockService (the inc-07 doc states '08 is therefore built before 07'). The lab/rad/proc report endpoints belong to Reporting (inc-10). Therefore the true topological NEXT full bounded-context build after inc-05 is Pharmacy, Inventory & Procurement (inc-08): it depends on master-data (inc-02), billing (inc-04), and is a prerequisite for inpatient (inc-07). Recommended sequence: inc-06A clinical-order top-up (closes inc-05 deferrals) → inc-08 (pharmacy/inventory/procurement) → inc-07 (inpatient/nursing, which then folds in the admission-scoped L/R/P paths) → inc-09 (hr/payroll/assets) → inc-10 (reporting). Run discovery-before-code on inc-08 and inc-07 first; the legacy survey shows both their planning docs carry the same class of drift (WardDayAccrualJob/ShedLock, two-approver discharge gate, MAR/FluidBalance/CareActivity, six-state prescription lifecycle, per-line pay-status sell gate, ThreeWayMatch/SupplierInvoice, StockMovement enum ledger — all flagged ABSENT in legacy).

ITEM 5 attachment storage backend: pin ADR-0015's FIRST concrete implementation here (S3-compatible object storage + virus-scan + PHI-at-rest encryption — net-new capability requiring the storage backend, scan tooling, and bucket/IaC decisions), OR ship legacy-parity local-disk streaming + 10 MiB cap now and defer object storage to a later cross-cutting increment? ITEM 4 (post-VERIFIED report amendment) is a confirmed legacy ASYMMETRY (report narrative overwritable after VERIFIED while result/range/level/unit are not). Confirm via legacy-analyst + healthcare-domain-expert whether this is intended-to-reproduce (it is a clinical safety / amendment-trail concern: overwriting a verified report without a versioned amendment trail) or a legacy defect we should NOT reproduce. If reproduced, do we add an audit-trail/amendment-version requirement (net-new, ADR-0007 scope) on top of the legacy behaviour?

ITEMs 2/4 require the clinical module to read billing BILL status (PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED) at report time — today clinical holds only a local 'settled' flag and by ADR-0008 §6 never reads billing bill status post-hoc. Approve adding a narrow read seam to billing.api (e.g. a bill-status query keyed by billUid) for the bill-gated add_report path, or instruct that the existing settled-flag projection is sufficient (risk: settled-flag was derived at order time and may not reflect a later cash payment, so it may not match the legacy add_report bill-status gate exactly)?

NEW-SCOPE register: do you want any of the rejected modern capabilities (generic attachment + object storage + ClamAV, tamper-evident domain-event audit on clinical transitions, RBAC privilege codes on clinical lifecycle endpoints, lab batch grouping, per-analyte results + reference-range flagging) opened as explicit change-requests with acceptance criteria written as NET-NEW (not legacy reproduction), or parked entirely? scope.rationale: The reconciliation is correct and I verified its load-bearing claims against the as-built code. inc-05 already shipped the full L/R/P/Theatre order+result+attachment+worklist loops under legacy-accurate names (three separate tables, VERIFIED terminal, COLLECTED step, HOLD revert, per-type attachments with VERIFIED delete-lock, settled-flag worklist filter, no-APPROVED procedure, Theatre-as-masterdata). The inc-06 planning doc is ~80% already-shipped or phantom (ClinicalOrder, LabResultLine + server-side flags, LabBatch + seq_lab_batch_no, OperativeRecord, procedure APPROVED/surgeon-approval, theatre scheduling, COMPLETED/CANCELLED states, ORDER_NOT_SETTLED hard gate, MinIO/S3/ClamAV-as-parity). So inc-06 is NOT a full build increment. The decisive new finding from reading the code: the single biggest genuine gap (credit-note + invoice reversal on L/R/P delete) was deferred in inc-05 because 'billing.api does not yet publish a cancel/credit-note command' — but that seam HAS since landed (BillingCommands.cancelCharge at billing/api/BillingCommands.java:58; CreditNoteService.cancelCharge implements the exact legacy reversal). The three inc-05 TODOs (LabTestService.java:449-450, ProcedureService.java:349, RadiologyService equivalent) are now stale and can be closed immediately. That makes the remainder a thin, mostly-SMALL clinical-order top-up (six items, the LARGE admission-scoped path correctly excluded to inc-07, reports to inc-10). The true next full bounded-context build is Pharmacy/Inventory/Procurement (inc-08), confirmed by the inc-07 doc itself stating '08 is therefore built before 07'. Per the locked discovery-before-code pattern, the inc-06 doc should be marked SUPERSEDED IN PART and re-authored from verified extractions before any code; acceptance criteria must come from the legacy extractions (exact states, exact error strings, exact bill-status gates), never from the drifted doc. I withheld any decision that depends on unverified legacy facts (the post-VERIFIED report amendment asymmetry is gated on legacy-analyst/HDE confirmation) and flagged the object-storage and clinical-bill-status-read items as architecture decisions the owner must ratify.
